ACRO_BAL_PITCH.description: rate at which pitch angle returns to level in acro and sport mode.  A higher value causes the vehicle to return to level faster. For helicopter sets the decay rate of the virtual flybar in the pitch axis. A higher value causes faster decay of desired to actual attitude.

ACRO_BAL_ROLL.description: rate at which roll angle returns to level in acro and sport mode.  A higher value causes the vehicle to return to level faster. For helicopter sets the decay rate of the virtual flybar in the roll axis. A higher value causes faster decay of desired to actual attitude.

ACRO_OPTIONS.description: A range of options that can be applied to change acro mode behaviour. Air-mode enables ATC_THR_MIX_MAN at all times (air-mode has no effect on helicopters). Rate Loop Only disables the use of angle stabilization and uses angular rate stabilization only.

ACRO_RP_EXPO.description: Acro roll/pitch Expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

ACRO_RP_RATE.description: Acro mode maximum roll and pitch rate.  Higher values mean faster rate of rotation

ACRO_RP_RATE_TC.displayName: Acro roll/pitch rate control input time constant
ACRO_RP_RATE_TC.description: Acro roll and pitch rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

ACRO_TRAINER.description: Type of trainer used in acro mode

ACRO_Y_EXPO.description: Acro yaw expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

ACRO_Y_RATE.description: Acro mode maximum yaw rate.  Higher value means faster rate of rotation

ACRO_Y_RATE_TC.displayName: Acro yaw rate control input time constant
ACRO_Y_RATE_TC.description: Acro yaw rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

ADSB_EMIT_TYPE.description: ADSB classification for the type of vehicle emitting the transponder signal. Default value is 14 (UAV).

ADSB_ICAO_ID.description: ICAO_ID unique vehicle identification number of this aircraft. This is an integer limited to 24bits. If set to 0 then one will be randomly generated. If set to -1 then static information is not sent, transceiver is assumed pre-programmed.

ADSB_ICAO_SPECL.description: ICAO_ID of special vehicle that ignores ADSB_LIST_RADIUS and ADSB_LIST_ALT. The vehicle is always tracked. Use 0 to disable.

ADSB_LEN_WIDTH.description: Aircraft length and width dimension options in Length and Width in meters. In most cases, use a value of 1 for smallest size.

ADSB_LIST_ALT.description: ADSB vehicle list altitude filter. Vehicles detected more than this altitude above our own altitude will be completely ignored. They will not show up in the SRx_ADSB stream to the GCS and will not be considered in any avoidance calculations. A value of 0 will disable this filter.

ADSB_LIST_MAX.description: ADSB list size of nearest vehicles. Longer lists take longer to refresh with lower SRx_ADSB values.

ADSB_LIST_RADIUS.description: ADSB vehicle list radius filter. Vehicles detected outside this radius will be completely ignored. They will not show up in the SRx_ADSB stream to the GCS and will not be considered in any avoidance calculations. A value of 0 will disable this filter.

ADSB_LOG.description: 0: no logging, 1: log only special ID, 2:log all

ADSB_OFFSET_LAT.description: GPS antenna lateral offset. This describes the physical location offset from center of the GPS antenna on the aircraft.

ADSB_OFFSET_LON.description: GPS antenna longitudinal offset. This is usually set to 1, Applied By Sensor

ADSB_OPTIONS.description: Options for emergency failsafe codes and device capabilities

ADSB_RF_CAPABLE.description: Describes your hardware RF In/Out capabilities.

ADSB_RF_SELECT.description: Transceiver RF selection for Rx enable and/or Tx enable. This only effects devices that can Tx and/or Rx. Rx-only devices should override this to always be Rx-only.

ADSB_SQUAWK.description: VFR squawk (Mode 3/A) code is a pre-programmed default code when the pilot is flying VFR and not in contact with ATC. In the USA, the VFR squawk code is octal 1200 (hex 0x280, decimal 640) and in most parts of Europe the VFR squawk code is octal 7000. If an invalid octal number is set then it will be reset to 1200.

ADSB_TYPE.description: Type of ADS-B hardware for ADSB-in and ADSB-out configuration and operation. If any type is selected then MAVLink based ADSB-in messages will always be enabled

AEROM_ALT_ABORT.description: Maximum allowable loss in altitude during a trick or sequence from its starting altitude.

AEROM_ANG_TC.description: This is the time over which we filter the desired roll to smooth it

AEROM_DEBUG.description: This controls the printing of extra debug information on paths

AEROM_ENTRY_RATE.displayName: The roll rate to use when entering a roll maneuver
AEROM_ENTRY_RATE.description: This controls how rapidly we roll into a new orientation

AEROM_ERR_COR_D.displayName: D gain for path error corrections
AEROM_ERR_COR_D.description: This controls how rapidly we correct back onto the desired path

AEROM_ERR_COR_P.displayName: P gain for path error corrections
AEROM_ERR_COR_P.description: This controls how rapidly we correct back onto the desired path

AEROM_KE_RUDD.description: Percent of rudder normally uses to sustain knife-edge at trick speed

AEROM_KE_RUDD_LK.description: Time to look ahead in the path to calculate rudder correction for bank angle

AEROM_LKAHD.description: This is how much time to look ahead in the path for calculating path rates

AEROM_MIS_ANGLE.description: When set to a non-zero value, this is the assumed direction of the mission. Otherwise the waypoint angle is used

AEROM_PATH_SCALE.description: Scale factor for Path/Box size. 0.5 would half the distances in maneuvers. Radii are unaffected.

AEROM_ROL_COR_TC.description: This is the time constant for correcting roll errors. A smaller value leads to faster roll corrections

AEROM_SPD_I.description: This controls how rapidly the throttle is raised to compensate for a speed error

AEROM_SPD_P.description: This controls how rapidly the throttle is raised to compensate for a speed error

AEROM_STALL_PIT.description: Pitch threashold for moving to final stage of stall turn

AEROM_STALL_THR.description: Amount of throttle to reduce to for a stall turn

AEROM_THR_BOOST.description: This is the extra throttle added in schedule elements marked as needing a throttle boost

AEROM_THR_LKAHD.description: This controls how far ahead we look in time along the path for the target throttle

AEROM_THR_PIT_FF.description: This controls how much extra throttle to add based on pitch ange. The value is for 90 degrees and is applied in proportion to pitch

AEROM_TIME_COR_P.displayName: Time constant for correction of our distance along the path
AEROM_TIME_COR_P.description: This is the time constant for correcting path position errors

AEROM_TS_I.description: This controls how rapidly two aircraft are brought back into time sync

AEROM_TS_P.description: This controls how rapidly two aircraft are brought back into time sync

AEROM_TS_RATE.displayName: Timesync rate of send of NAMED_VALUE_FLOAT data
AEROM_TS_RATE.description: This sets the rate we send data for time sync between aircraft

AEROM_TS_SPDMAX.description: This sets the maximum speed adjustment for time sync between aircraft

AEROM_YAW_ACCEL.description: This is maximum yaw acceleration to use

AFS_AMSL_ERR_GPS.displayName: Error margin for GPS based AMSL limit
AFS_AMSL_ERR_GPS.description: This sets margin for error in GPS derived altitude limit. This error margin is only used if the barometer has failed. If the barometer fails then the GPS will be used to enforce the AMSL_LIMIT, but this margin will be subtracted from the AMSL_LIMIT first, to ensure that even with the given amount of GPS altitude error the pressure altitude is not breached. OBC users should set this to comply with their D2 safety case. A value of -1 will mean that barometer failure will lead to immediate termination.

AFS_AMSL_LIMIT.description: This sets the AMSL (above mean sea level) altitude limit. If the pressure altitude determined by QNH exceeds this limit then flight termination will be forced. Note that this limit is in meters, whereas pressure altitude limits are often quoted in feet. A value of zero disables the pressure altitude limit.

AFS_DUAL_LOSS.displayName: Enable dual loss terminate due to failure of both GCS and GPS simultaneously
AFS_DUAL_LOSS.description: This enables the dual loss termination part of the AFS system. If this parameter is 1 and both GPS and the ground control station fail simultaneously, this will be considered a "dual loss" and cause termination.

AFS_ENABLE.description: This enables the advanced failsafe system. If this is set to zero (disable) then all the other AFS options have no effect

AFS_GCS_TIMEOUT.description: The time (in seconds) of persistent data link loss before GCS failsafe occurs.

AFS_GEOFENCE.description: This enables the geofence part of the AFS. Will only be in effect if AFS_ENABLE is also 1

AFS_HB_PIN.description: This sets a digital output pin which is cycled at 10Hz when termination is not activated. Note that if a FS_TERM_PIN is set then the heartbeat pin will continue to cycle at 10Hz when termination is activated, to allow the termination board to distinguish between autopilot crash and termination. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

AFS_MAN_PIN.description: This sets a digital output pin to set high when in manual mode.  See the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

AFS_MAX_COM_LOSS.displayName: Maximum number of comms loss events
AFS_MAX_COM_LOSS.description: Maximum number of comms loss events before the aircraft stops returning to mission on comms recovery. Use zero to allow for any number of comms loss events.

AFS_MAX_GPS_LOSS.displayName: Maximum number of GPS loss events
AFS_MAX_GPS_LOSS.description: Maximum number of GPS loss events before the aircraft stops returning to mission on GPS recovery. Use zero to allow for any number of GPS loss events.

AFS_MAX_RANGE.description: This is the maximum range of the vehicle in kilometers from first arming. If the vehicle goes beyond this range then the TERM_ACTION is performed. A value of zero disables this feature.

AFS_OPTIONS.description: See description for each bitmask bit description

AFS_QNH_PRESSURE.description: This sets the QNH pressure in millibars to be used for pressure altitude in the altitude limit. A value of zero disables the altitude limit.

AFS_RC.description: This enables the RC part of the AFS. Will only be in effect if AFS_ENABLE is also 1

AFS_RC_FAIL_TIME.description: This is the time in seconds in manual mode that failsafe termination will activate if RC input is lost. For the OBC rules this should be (1.5). Use 0 to disable.

AFS_RC_MAN_ONLY.displayName: Enable RC Termination only in manual control modes
AFS_RC_MAN_ONLY.description: If this parameter is set to 1, then an RC loss will only cause the plane to terminate in manual control modes. If it is 0, then the plane will terminate in any flight mode.

AFS_TERM_ACTION.description: This can be used to force an action on flight termination. Normally this is handled by an external failsafe board, but you can setup ArduPilot to handle it here. Please consult the wiki for more information on the possible values of the parameter

AFS_TERM_PIN.description: This sets a digital output pin to set high on flight termination. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

AFS_TERMINATE.description: Can be set in flight to force termination of the heartbeat signal

AFS_WP_COMMS.description: Waypoint number to navigate to on comms loss

AFS_WP_GPS_LOSS.description: Waypoint number to navigate to on GPS lock loss

AHRS_COMP_BETA.displayName: AHRS Velocity Complementary Filter Beta Coefficient
AHRS_COMP_BETA.description: This controls the time constant for the cross-over frequency used to fuse AHRS (airspeed and heading) and GPS data to estimate ground velocity. Time constant is 0.1/beta. A larger time constant will use GPS data less and a small time constant will use air data less.

AHRS_CUSTOM_PIT.description: Autopilot mounting position pitch offset. Positive values = pitch up, negative values = pitch down. This parameter is only used when AHRS_ORIENTATION is set to CUSTOM.

AHRS_CUSTOM_ROLL.description: Autopilot mounting position roll offset. Positive values = roll right, negative values = roll left. This parameter is only used when AHRS_ORIENTATION is set to CUSTOM.

AHRS_CUSTOM_YAW.description: Autopilot mounting position yaw offset. Positive values = yaw right, negative values = yaw left. This parameter is only used when AHRS_ORIENTATION is set to CUSTOM.

AHRS_EKF_TYPE.displayName: Use NavEKF Kalman filter for attitude and position estimation
AHRS_EKF_TYPE.description: This controls which NavEKF Kalman filter version is used for attitude and position estimation

AHRS_GPS_GAIN.description: This controls how much to use the GPS to correct the attitude. This should never be set to zero for a plane as it would result in the plane losing control in turns. For a plane please use the default value of 1.0.

AHRS_GPS_MINSATS.description: Minimum number of satellites visible to use GPS for velocity based corrections attitude correction. This defaults to 6, which is about the point at which the velocity numbers from a GPS become too unreliable for accurate correction of the accelerometers.

AHRS_GPS_USE.displayName: AHRS use GPS for DCM navigation and position-down
AHRS_GPS_USE.description: This controls whether to use dead-reckoning or GPS based navigation. If set to 0 then the GPS won't be used for navigation, and only dead reckoning will be used. A value of zero should never be used for normal flight. Currently this affects only the DCM-based AHRS: the EKF uses GPS according to its own parameters. A value of 2 means to use GPS for height as well as position - both in DCM estimation and when determining altitude-above-home.

values.label: Use GPS for DCM position and height

AHRS_OPTIONS.description: This controls optional AHRS behaviour. Setting DisableDCMFallbackFW will change the AHRS behaviour for fixed wing aircraft in fly-forward flight to not fall back to DCM when the EKF stops navigating. Setting DisableDCMFallbackVTOL will change the AHRS behaviour for fixed wing aircraft in non fly-forward (VTOL) flight to not fall back to DCM when the EKF stops navigating. Setting DontDisableAirspeedUsingEKF disables the EKF based innovation check for airspeed consistency

AHRS_ORIENTATION.description: Overall board orientation relative to the standard orientation for the board type. This rotates the IMU and compass readings to allow the board to be oriented in your vehicle at any 90 or 45 degree angle. The label for each option is specified in the order of rotations for that orientation. This option takes affect on next boot. After changing you will need to re-level your vehicle. Firmware versions 4.2 and prior can use a CUSTOM (100) rotation to set the AHRS_CUSTOM_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles for AHRS orientation. Later versions provide two general custom rotations which can be used, Custom 1 and Custom 2, with CUST_ROT1_ROLL/PIT/YAW or CUST_ROT2_ROLL/PIT/YAW angles.

AHRS_RP_P.description: This controls how fast the accelerometers correct the attitude

AHRS_TRIM_X.description: Compensates for the roll angle difference between the control board and the frame. Positive values make the vehicle roll right.

AHRS_TRIM_Y.description: Compensates for the pitch angle difference between the control board and the frame. Positive values make the vehicle pitch up/back.

AHRS_WIND_MAX.description: This sets the maximum allowable difference between ground speed and airspeed. A value of zero means to use the airspeed as is. This allows the plane to cope with a failing airspeed sensor by clipping it to groundspeed plus/minus this limit. See ARSPD_OPTIONS and ARSPD_WIND_MAX to disable airspeed sensors.

AHRS_YAW_P.description: This controls the weight the compass or GPS has on the heading. A higher value means the heading will track the yaw source (GPS or compass) more rapidly.

AIS_LIST_MAX.description: AIS list size of nearest vessels. Longer lists take longer to refresh with lower SRx_ADSB values.

AIS_TIME_OUT.description: if no updates are received in this time a vessel will be removed from the list

ANGLE_MAX.description: Maximum lean angle in all flight modes

ARM_C_RTL_ALT.displayName: RTL_ALT should be a valid value
ARM_C_RTL_ALT.description: RTL_ALT should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_ESTOP.description: Emergency Stop disables arming. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FENCE.description: Fences loaded but no fence enabled. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FOLL_OFS_DEF.description: Follow offsets should not be left as default (zero) if FOLL_ENABLE = 1. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FOLL_SYSID.description: If FOLL_ENABLE = 1, check that FOLL_SYSID has been set. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_FOLL_SYSID_X.description: If FOLL_ENABLE = 1, check that FOLL_SYSID is different to MAV_SYSID. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_MNTX_SYSID.displayName: Follow and Mount should follow the same vehicle
ARM_MNTX_SYSID.description: If FOLL_ENABLE = 1 and MNTx_SYSID_DEFLT is set, check that FOLL_SYSID is equal MNTx. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_AIRSPEED.description: Validate that AIRSPEED_STALL(if set) < MIN < CRUISE < MAX d. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_Q_FS_LAND.displayName: Warn if Q failsafe will land
ARM_P_Q_FS_LAND.description: Notify the user that on failsafe a QuadPlan will land. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_Q_FS_RTL.displayName: Warn if Q failsafe will QRTL
ARM_P_Q_FS_RTL.description: Notify the user that on failsafe a QuadPlan will QRTL. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_QRTL_ALT.displayName: Q_RTL_ALT should be a valid value
ARM_P_QRTL_ALT.description: Q_RTL_ALT should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_RTL_ALT.displayName: RTL_ALTITUDE should be a valid value
ARM_P_RTL_ALT.description: RTL_ALTITITUDE should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_SCALING.description: Validate that SCALING_SPEED is within 20% of AIRSPEED_CRUISE. If SCALING_SPEED changes the vehicle may need to be retuned. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_P_STALL.description: Validate that AIRSPEED_MIN is at least 25% above AIRSPEED_STALL(if set). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_RALLY.description: Rally Point more than RALLY_LIMIT_KM kilometers away. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_RTL_CLIMB.displayName: RTL_CLIMB_MIN should be a valid value
ARM_RTL_CLIMB.description: RTL_CLIMB_MIN should be < 120m (400ft). 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_SYSID.description: Check that MAV_SYSID (or SYDID_THISMAV) has been set. 3 or less to prevent arming. -1 to disable.

ARM_V_ALT_LEGAL.description: Legal max altitude for UAV/RPAS/drones in your jurisdiction

ARMING_ACCTHRESH.description: Accelerometer error threshold used to determine inconsistent accelerometers. Compares this error range to other accelerometers to detect a hardware or calibration error. Lower value means tighter check and harder to pass arming check. Not all accelerometers are created equal.

ARMING_CHECK.description: Checks prior to arming motor. This is a bitmask of checks that will be performed before allowing arming. For most users it is recommended to leave this at the default of 1 (all checks enabled). You can select whatever checks you prefer by adding together the values of each check type to set this parameter. For example, to only allow arming when you have GPS lock and no RC failsafe you would set ARMING_CHECK to 72.

ARMING_CRSDP_IGN.description: Must have value "1" if crashdump data is present on the system, or a prearm failure will be raised.  Do not set this parameter unless the risks of doing so are fully understood.  The presence of a crash dump means that the firmware currently installed has suffered a critical software failure which resulted in the autopilot immediately rebooting.  The crashdump file gives diagnostic information which can help in finding the issue, please contact the ArduPIlot support team.  If this crashdump data is present, the vehicle is likely unsafe to fly.  Check the ArduPilot documentation for more details.

ARMING_MAGTHRESH.displayName: Compass magnetic field strength error threshold vs earth magnetic model
ARMING_MAGTHRESH.description: Compass magnetic field strength error threshold vs earth magnetic model.  X and y axis are compared using this threhold, Z axis uses 2x this threshold.  0 to disable check

ARMING_MIS_ITEMS.description: Bitmask of mission items that are required to be planned in order to arm the aircraft

ARMING_NEED_LOC.description: Require that the vehicle have an absolute position before it arms.  This can help ensure that the vehicle can Return To Launch.

ARMING_OPTIONS.description: Options that can be applied to change arming behaviour

ARMING_RUDDER.description: Allow arm/disarm by rudder input. When enabled arming can be done with right rudder, disarming with left rudder. Rudder arming only works with throttle at zero +- deadzone (RCx_DZ). Depending on vehicle type, arming in certain modes is prevented. See the wiki for each vehicle. Caution is recommended when arming if it is allowed in an auto-throttle mode!

AROT_AS_ACC_MAX.description: Maximum forward acceleration to apply in speed controller.

AROT_COL_FILT_E.description: Cut-off frequency for collective low pass filter.  For the entry phase.  Acts as a following trim.  Must be higher than AROT_COL_FILT_G.

AROT_COL_FILT_G.description: Cut-off frequency for collective low pass filter.  For the glide phase.  Acts as a following trim.  Must be lower than AROT_COL_FILT_E.

AROT_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the autonomous autorotation capability.

AROT_FW_V_FF.description: Velocity (horizontal) input filter.  Corrects the target acceleration proportionally to the desired velocity.

AROT_FW_V_P.description: Velocity (horizontal) P gain.  Determines the proportion of the target acceleration based on the velocity error.

AROT_HS_P.displayName: P gain for head speed controller
AROT_HS_P.description: Increase value to increase sensitivity of head speed controller during autonomous autorotation.

AROT_HS_SENSOR.description: Allocate the RPM sensor instance to use for measuring head speed.  RPM1 = 0.  RPM2 = 1.

AROT_HS_SET_PT.description: The target head speed in RPM during autorotation.  Start by setting to desired hover speed and tune from there as necessary.

AROT_TARG_SP.description: Target ground speed in cm/s for the autorotation controller to try and achieve/ maintain during the glide phase.

ARSPD_AUTOCAL.displayName: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.
ARSPD_AUTOCAL.description: Enables automatic adjustment of airspeed ratio during a calibration flight based on estimation of ground speed and true airspeed. New ratio saved every 2 minutes if change is > 5%. Should not be left enabled.

ARSPD_BUS.description: Bus number of the I2C bus where the airspeed sensor is connected. May not correspond to board's I2C bus number labels. Retry another bus and reboot if airspeed sensor fails to initialize.

ARSPD_DEVID.description: Airspeed sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

ARSPD_OFF_PCNT.description: The maximum percentage speed change in airspeed reports that is allowed due to offset changes between calibrations before a warning is issued. This potential speed error is in percent of ASPD_FBW_MIN. 0 disables. Helps warn of calibrations without pitot being covered.

ARSPD_OPTIONS.description: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD_PIN.description: The pin number that the airspeed sensor is connected to for analog sensors. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

ARSPD_PRIMARY.description: This selects which airspeed sensor will be the primary if multiple sensors are found

ARSPD_PSI_RANGE.displayName: The PSI range of the device
ARSPD_PSI_RANGE.description: This parameter allows you to set the PSI (pounds per square inch) range for your sensor. You should not change this unless you examine the datasheet for your device

ARSPD_RATIO.description: Calibrates pitot tube pressure to velocity. Increasing this value will indicate a higher airspeed at any given dynamic pressure.

ARSPD_SKIP_CAL.displayName: Skip airspeed offset calibration on startup
ARSPD_SKIP_CAL.description: This parameter allows you to skip airspeed offset calibration on startup, instead using the offset from the last calibration. This may be desirable if the offset variance between flights for your sensor is low and you want to avoid having to cover the pitot tube on each boot.

ARSPD_TUBE_ORDER.description: This parameter allows you to control whether the order in which the tubes are attached to your pitot tube matters. If you set this to 0 then the first (often the top) connector on the sensor needs to be the stagnation pressure (the pressure at the tip of the pitot tube). If set to 1 then the second (often the bottom) connector needs to be the stagnation pressure. If set to 2 (the default) then the airspeed driver will accept either order. The reason you may wish to specify the order is it will allow your airspeed sensor to detect if the aircraft is receiving excessive pressure on the static port compared to the stagnation port such as during a stall, which would otherwise be seen as a positive airspeed.

ARSPD_TUBE_ORDR.description: This parameter allows you to control whether the order in which the tubes are attached to your pitot tube matters. If you set this to 0 then the first (often the top) connector on the sensor needs to be the stagnation pressure (the pressure at the tip of the pitot tube). If set to 1 then the second (often the bottom) connector needs to be the stagnation pressure. If set to 2 (the default) then the airspeed driver will accept either order. The reason you may wish to specify the order is it will allow your airspeed sensor to detect if the aircraft is receiving excessive pressure on the static port compared to the stagnation port such as during a stall, which would otherwise be seen as a positive airspeed.

ARSPD_USE.description: This parameter is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD_WIND_GATE.description: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle.

ARSPD_WIND_MAX.displayName: Maximum airspeed and ground speed difference
ARSPD_WIND_MAX.description: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD_WIND_WARN.displayName: Airspeed and GPS speed difference that gives a warning
ARSPD_WIND_WARN.description: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ARSPD2_AUTOCAL.displayName: This parameter and function is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.
ARSPD2_AUTOCAL.description: Enables automatic adjustment of airspeed ratio during a calibration flight based on estimation of ground speed and true airspeed. New ratio saved every 2 minutes if change is > 5%. Should not be left enabled.

ARSPD2_BUS.description: Bus number of the I2C bus where the airspeed sensor is connected. May not correspond to board's I2C bus number labels. Retry another bus and reboot if airspeed sensor fails to initialize.

ARSPD2_DEVID.description: Airspeed sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

ARSPD2_PIN.description: The pin number that the airspeed sensor is connected to for analog sensors. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

ARSPD2_PSI_RANGE.displayName: The PSI range of the device
ARSPD2_PSI_RANGE.description: This parameter allows you to set the PSI (pounds per square inch) range for your sensor. You should not change this unless you examine the datasheet for your device

ARSPD2_RATIO.description: Calibrates pitot tube pressure to velocity. Increasing this value will indicate a higher airspeed at any given dynamic pressure.

ARSPD2_SKIP_CAL.displayName: Skip airspeed offset calibration on startup
ARSPD2_SKIP_CAL.description: This parameter allows you to skip airspeed offset calibration on startup, instead using the offset from the last calibration. This may be desirable if the offset variance between flights for your sensor is low and you want to avoid having to cover the pitot tube on each boot.

ARSPD2_TUBE_ORDR.description: This parameter allows you to control whether the order in which the tubes are attached to your pitot tube matters. If you set this to 0 then the first (often the top) connector on the sensor needs to be the stagnation pressure (the pressure at the tip of the pitot tube). If set to 1 then the second (often the bottom) connector needs to be the stagnation pressure. If set to 2 (the default) then the airspeed driver will accept either order. The reason you may wish to specify the order is it will allow your airspeed sensor to detect if the aircraft is receiving excessive pressure on the static port compared to the stagnation port such as during a stall, which would otherwise be seen as a positive airspeed.

ARSPD2_USE.description: This parameter is not used by this vehicle. Always set to 0.

ATC_ANG_LIM_TC.displayName: Angle Limit (to maintain altitude) Time Constant
ATC_ANG_LIM_TC.description: Angle Limit (to maintain altitude) Time Constant

ATC_ANG_PIT_P.displayName: Pitch axis angle controller P gain
ATC_ANG_PIT_P.description: Pitch axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired pitch angle and actual angle to a desired pitch rate

ATC_ANG_RLL_P.displayName: Roll axis angle controller P gain
ATC_ANG_RLL_P.description: Roll axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired roll angle and actual angle to a desired roll rate

ATC_ANG_YAW_P.displayName: Yaw axis angle controller P gain
ATC_ANG_YAW_P.description: Yaw axis angle controller P gain.  Converts the error between the desired yaw angle and actual angle to a desired yaw rate

ATC_ANGLE_BOOST.description: Angle Boost increases output throttle as the vehicle leans to reduce loss of altitude

ATC_HOVR_ROL_TRM.description: Trim the hover roll angle to counter tail rotor thrust in a hover

ATC_INPUT_TC.description: Attitude control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

ATC_LAND_P_MULT.description: Pitch gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the pitch axis.

ATC_LAND_R_MULT.description: Roll gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the roll axis.

ATC_LAND_Y_MULT.description: Yaw gain multiplier active when landed. A factor of 1.0 means no reduction in gain while landed. Reduce this factor to reduce ground oscitation in the yaw axis.

ATC_RAT_PIT_D.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller D gain
ATC_RAT_PIT_D.description: Pitch axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

ATC_RAT_PIT_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

ATC_RAT_PIT_FF.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller feed forward
ATC_RAT_PIT_FF.description: Pitch axis rate controller feed forward

ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTD.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTD.description: Pitch axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTE.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTE.description: Pitch axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTT.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_PIT_FLTT.description: Pitch axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_PIT_I.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller I gain
ATC_RAT_PIT_I.description: Pitch axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

ATC_RAT_PIT_ILMI.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller I-term leak minimum
ATC_RAT_PIT_ILMI.description: Point below which I-term will not leak down

ATC_RAT_PIT_IMAX.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum
ATC_RAT_PIT_IMAX.description: Pitch axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

ATC_RAT_PIT_P.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller P gain
ATC_RAT_PIT_P.description: Pitch axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired pitch rate vs actual pitch rate

ATC_RAT_PIT_PDMX.displayName: Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum
ATC_RAT_PIT_PDMX.description: Pitch axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

ATC_RAT_PIT_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

ATC_RAT_RLL_D.displayName: Roll axis rate controller D gain
ATC_RAT_RLL_D.description: Roll axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

ATC_RAT_RLL_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

ATC_RAT_RLL_FF.displayName: Roll axis rate controller feed forward
ATC_RAT_RLL_FF.description: Roll axis rate controller feed forward

ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTD.displayName: Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTD.description: Roll axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTE.displayName: Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTE.description: Roll axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTT.displayName: Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_RLL_FLTT.description: Roll axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_RLL_I.displayName: Roll axis rate controller I gain
ATC_RAT_RLL_I.description: Roll axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

ATC_RAT_RLL_ILMI.displayName: Roll axis rate controller I-term leak minimum
ATC_RAT_RLL_ILMI.description: Point below which I-term will not leak down

ATC_RAT_RLL_IMAX.displayName: Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum
ATC_RAT_RLL_IMAX.description: Roll axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

ATC_RAT_RLL_P.displayName: Roll axis rate controller P gain
ATC_RAT_RLL_P.description: Roll axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired roll rate vs actual roll rate

ATC_RAT_RLL_PDMX.displayName: Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum
ATC_RAT_RLL_PDMX.description: Roll axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

ATC_RAT_RLL_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

ATC_RAT_YAW_D.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller D gain
ATC_RAT_YAW_D.description: Yaw axis rate controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

ATC_RAT_YAW_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

ATC_RAT_YAW_FF.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward
ATC_RAT_YAW_FF.description: Yaw axis rate controller feed forward

ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTD.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTD.description: Yaw axis rate controller derivative frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTE.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTE.description: Yaw axis rate controller error frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTT.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz
ATC_RAT_YAW_FLTT.description: Yaw axis rate controller target frequency in Hz

ATC_RAT_YAW_I.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller I gain
ATC_RAT_YAW_I.description: Yaw axis rate controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

ATC_RAT_YAW_ILMI.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller I-term leak minimum
ATC_RAT_YAW_ILMI.description: Point below which I-term will not leak down

ATC_RAT_YAW_IMAX.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum
ATC_RAT_YAW_IMAX.description: Yaw axis rate controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum that the I term will output

ATC_RAT_YAW_P.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller P gain
ATC_RAT_YAW_P.description: Yaw axis rate controller P gain.  Corrects in proportion to the difference between the desired yaw rate vs actual yaw rate

ATC_RAT_YAW_PDMX.displayName: Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum
ATC_RAT_YAW_PDMX.description: Yaw axis rate controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

ATC_RAT_YAW_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

ATC_RATE_FF_ENAB.description: Controls whether body-frame rate feedforward is enabled or disabled

ATC_RATE_P_MAX.description: Maximum angular velocity in pitch axis

ATC_RATE_R_MAX.description: Maximum angular velocity in roll axis

ATC_RATE_Y_MAX.description: Maximum angular velocity in yaw axis

ATC_SLEW_YAW.description: Maximum rate the yaw target can be updated in RTL and Auto flight modes

ATC_THR_G_BOOST.description: Throttle-gain boost ratio. A value of 0 means no boosting is applied, a value of 1 means full boosting is applied. Describes the ratio increase that is applied to angle P and PD on pitch and roll.

ATC_THR_MIX_MAN.description: Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used during manual flight (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)

ATC_THR_MIX_MAX.description: Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used during active flight (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)

ATC_THR_MIX_MIN.description: Throttle vs attitude control prioritisation used when landing (higher values mean we prioritise attitude control over throttle)

AUTO_OPTIONS.description: A range of options that can be applied to change auto mode behaviour. Allow Arming allows the copter to be armed in Auto. Allow Takeoff Without Raising Throttle allows takeoff without the pilot having to raise the throttle. Ignore pilot yaw overrides the pilot's yaw stick being used while in auto.

AUTOTUNE_ACC_MAX.description: maximum angular acceleration in deg/s/s allowed during autotune maneuvers

AUTOTUNE_AGGR.description: Autotune aggressiveness. Defines the bounce back used to detect size of the D term.

AUTOTUNE_FRQ_MAX.description: Defines the end frequency for sweeps and dwells

AUTOTUNE_FRQ_MIN.description: Defines the start frequency for sweeps and dwells

AUTOTUNE_GN_MAX.description: Defines the response gain (output/input) to tune

AUTOTUNE_RAT_MAX.description: maximum angular rate in deg/s allowed during autotune maneuvers

AUTOTUNE_SEQ.description: 2-byte bitmask to select what tuning should be performed.  Max gain automatically performed if Rate D is selected. Values: 7:All,1:VFF Only,2:Rate D/Rate P Only(incl max gain),4:Angle P Only,8:Max Gain Only,16:Tune Check,3:VFF and Rate D/Rate P(incl max gain),5:VFF and Angle P,6:Rate D/Rate P(incl max gain) and angle P

AUTOTUNE_VELXY_P.description: Velocity xy P gain used to hold position during Max Gain, Rate P, and Rate D frequency sweeps

AVD_F_ACTION.description: Specifies aircraft behaviour when a collision is imminent

AVD_F_ALT_MIN.description: Minimum AMSL (above mean sea level) altitude for ADS-B avoidance. If the vehicle is below this altitude, no avoidance action will take place. Useful to prevent ADS-B avoidance from activating while below the tree line or around structures. Default of 0 is no minimum.

AVD_F_DIST_XY.description: Closest allowed projected distance before F_ACTION is undertaken

AVD_F_DIST_Z.description: Closest allowed projected distance before BEHAVIOUR_F is undertaken

AVD_F_RCVRY.displayName: Recovery behaviour after a fail event
AVD_F_RCVRY.description: Determines what the aircraft will do after a fail event is resolved

AVD_F_TIME.description: Aircraft velocity vectors are multiplied by this time to determine closest approach.  If this results in an approach closer than F_DIST_XY or F_DIST_Z then F_ACTION is undertaken

AVD_OBS_MAX.displayName: Maximum number of obstacles to track
AVD_OBS_MAX.description: Maximum number of obstacles to track

AVD_W_ACTION.description: Specifies aircraft behaviour when a collision may occur

AVD_W_DIST_XY.description: Closest allowed projected distance before W_ACTION is undertaken

AVD_W_DIST_Z.description: Closest allowed projected distance before BEHAVIOUR_W is undertaken

AVD_W_TIME.description: Aircraft velocity vectors are multiplied by this time to determine closest approach.  If this results in an approach closer than W_DIST_XY or W_DIST_Z then W_ACTION is undertaken (assuming F_ACTION is not undertaken)

AVOID_ACCEL_MAX.description: Maximum acceleration with which obstacles will be avoided with. Set zero to disable acceleration limits

AVOID_ALT_MIN.description: Minimum altitude above which proximity based avoidance will start working. This requires a valid downward facing rangefinder reading to work. Set zero to disable

AVOID_ANGLE_MAX.displayName: Avoidance max lean angle in non-GPS flight modes
AVOID_ANGLE_MAX.description: Max lean angle used to avoid obstacles while in non-GPS modes

AVOID_BACKUP_DZ.displayName: Avoidance deadzone between stopping and backing away from obstacle
AVOID_BACKUP_DZ.description: Distance beyond AVOID_MARGIN parameter, after which vehicle will backaway from obstacles. Increase this parameter if you see vehicle going back and forth in front of obstacle.

AVOID_BACKUP_SPD.description: Maximum speed that will be used to back away from obstacles horizontally in position control modes (m/s). Set zero to disable horizontal backup.

AVOID_BACKZ_SPD.description: Maximum speed that will be used to back away from obstacles vertically in height control modes (m/s). Set zero to disable vertical backup.

AVOID_DIST_MAX.displayName: Avoidance distance maximum in non-GPS flight modes
AVOID_DIST_MAX.description: Distance from object at which obstacle avoidance will begin in non-GPS modes

AVOID_MARGIN.displayName: Avoidance distance margin in GPS modes
AVOID_MARGIN.description: Vehicle will attempt to stay at least this distance (in meters) from objects while in GPS modes

BARO_ALT_OFFSET.description: altitude offset in meters added to barometric altitude. This is used to allow for automatic adjustment of the base barometric altitude by a ground station equipped with a barometer. The value is added to the barometric altitude read by the aircraft. It is automatically reset to 0 when the barometer is calibrated on each reboot or when a preflight calibration is performed.

BARO_ALTERR_MAX.description: This is the maximum acceptable altitude discrepancy between GPS altitude and barometric presssure altitude calculated against a standard atmosphere for arming checks to pass. If you are getting an arming error due to this parameter then you may have a faulty or substituted barometer. A common issue is vendors replacing a MS5611 in a "Pixhawk" with a MS5607. If you have that issue then please see BARO_OPTIONS parameter to force the MS5611 to be treated as a MS5607. This check is disabled if the value is zero.

BARO_EXT_BUS.description: This selects the bus number for looking for an I2C barometer. When set to -1 it will probe all external i2c buses based on the BARO_PROBE_EXT parameter.

BARO_FIELD_ELV.description: User provided field elevation in meters. This is used to improve the calculation of the altitude the vehicle is at. This parameter is not persistent and will be reset to 0 every time the vehicle is rebooted. Changes to this parameter will only be used when disarmed. A value of 0 means the EKF origin height is used for takeoff height above sea level.

BARO_FLTR_RNG.displayName: Range in which sample is accepted
BARO_FLTR_RNG.description: This sets the range around the average value that new samples must be within to be accepted. This can help reduce the impact of noise on sensors that are on long I2C cables. The value is a percentage from the average value. A value of zero disables this filter.

BARO_GND_TEMP.description: User provided ambient ground temperature in degrees Celsius. This is used to improve the calculation of the altitude the vehicle is at. This parameter is not persistent and will be reset to 0 every time the vehicle is rebooted. A value of 0 means use the internal measurement ambient temperature.

BARO_PRIMARY.description: This selects which barometer will be the primary if multiple barometers are found

BARO_PROBE_EXT.description: This sets which types of external i2c barometer to look for. It is a bitmask of barometer types. The I2C buses to probe is based on BARO_EXT_BUS. If BARO_EXT_BUS is -1 then it will probe all external buses, otherwise it will probe just the bus number given in BARO_EXT_BUS.

BARO{idx}_WCF_BCK.displayName: Pressure error coefficient in negative X direction (backwards)
BARO{idx}_WCF_BCK.description: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during backwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO{idx}_WCF_DN.displayName: Pressure error coefficient in negative Z direction (down)
BARO{idx}_WCF_DN.description: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during descending flight (or forward flight with a high backwards lean angle, eg braking manoeuvre), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO{idx}_WCF_ENABLE.description: This enables the use of wind coefficients for barometer compensation

BARO{idx}_WCF_FWD.displayName: Pressure error coefficient in positive X direction (forward)
BARO{idx}_WCF_FWD.description: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the X body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during forwards flight, then this will be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO{idx}_WCF_LFT.displayName: Pressure error coefficient in negative Y direction (left)
BARO{idx}_WCF_LFT.description: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a negative wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the left, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO{idx}_WCF_RGT.displayName: Pressure error coefficient in positive Y direction (right)
BARO{idx}_WCF_RGT.description: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Y body axis. If the baro height estimate rises during sideways flight to the right, then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO{idx}_WCF_UP.displayName: Pressure error coefficient in positive Z direction (up)
BARO{idx}_WCF_UP.description: This is the ratio of static pressure error to dynamic pressure generated by a positive wind relative velocity along the Z body axis. If the baro height estimate rises above truth height during climbing flight (or forward flight with a high forwards lean angle), then this should be a negative number. Multirotors can use this feature only if using EKF3 and if the EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_X and EK3_DRAG_BCOEF_Y parameters have been tuned.

BARO1_DEVID.description: Barometer sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

BARO2_DEVID.description: Barometer2 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

BARO3_DEVID.description: Barometer3 sensor ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

BATT_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

BATT_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

BATT_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

BATT_ESC_INDEX.displayName: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

BATT_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATT_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

BATT_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.

BATT_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.

BATT_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

BATT{idx}_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

BATT{idx}_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

BATT{idx}_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATT{idx}_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATT{idx}_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATT{idx}_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

BATT{idx}_ESC_INDEX.displayName: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATT{idx}_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATT{idx}_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATT{idx}_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATT{idx}_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATT{idx}_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT{idx}_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATT{idx}_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATT{idx}_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATT{idx}_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATT{idx}_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATT{idx}_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATT{idx}_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

BATT{idx}_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATT{idx}_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATT{idx}_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

BATT{idx}_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATT{idx}_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATT{idx}_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATT{idx}_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.

BATT{idx}_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

BATT2_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATT2_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATT2_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT2_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATT2_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT2_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT2_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT2_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT2_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT2_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.

BATT9_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATT9_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATT9_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATT9_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATT9_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.

BATTA_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

BATTA_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

BATTA_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTA_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATTA_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATTA_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATTA_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTA_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTA_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTA_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATTA_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

BATTA_ESC_INDEX.displayName: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATTA_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTA_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATTA_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATTA_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATTA_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTA_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTA_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATTA_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATTA_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATTA_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATTA_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTA_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATTA_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTA_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTA_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTA_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

BATTA_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATTA_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATTA_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

BATTA_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATTA_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATTA_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATTA_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.

BATTA_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTA_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.

BATTA_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

BATTB_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

BATTB_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

BATTC_MAX_AMPS.description: This controls the maximum current the INS2XX sensor will work with.

BATTC_MAX_VOLT.description: Maximum voltage of battery. Provides scaling of current versus voltage

BATTC_MONITOR.description: Controls enabling monitoring of the battery's voltage and current

BATTC_OPTIONS.description: This sets options to change the behaviour of the battery monitor

BATTC_SERIAL_NUM.description: Battery serial number, automatically filled in for SMBus batteries, otherwise will be -1. With DroneCan it is the battery_id.

BATTC_SHUNT.description: This sets the shunt resistor used in the device

BATTC_SUM_MASK.description: 0: sum of remaining battery monitors, If none 0 sum of specified monitors. Current will be summed and voltages averaged.

BATTC_VLT_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset on voltage pin. This allows for an offset due to a diode. This voltage is subtracted before the scaling is applied.

BATTC_VOLT_MULT.description: Used to convert the voltage of the voltage sensing pin (BATTC_VOLT_PIN) to the actual battery's voltage (pin_voltage * VOLT_MULT). For the 3DR Power brick with a Pixhawk, this should be set to 10.1. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 12.02. For the PX using the PX4IO power supply this should be set to 1.

BATTC_VOLT_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for voltage monitoring.

BATTD_AMP_OFFSET.description: Voltage offset at zero current on current sensor for Analog Sensors. For Synthetic Current sensor, this offset is the zero throttle system current and is added to the calculated throttle base current.

BATTD_AMP_PERVLT.description: Number of amps that a 1V reading on the current sensor corresponds to. With a Pixhawk using the 3DR Power brick this should be set to 17. For the Pixhawk with the 3DR 4in1 ESC this should be 17. For Synthetic Current sensor monitors, this is the maximum, full throttle current draw.

BATTD_ARM_MAH.description: Battery capacity remaining which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any capacity. Note that execept for smart batteries rebooting the vehicle will always reset the remaining capacity estimate, which can lead to this check not providing sufficent protection, it is recommended to always use this in conjunction with the BATTD_ARM_VOLT parameter.

BATTD_ARM_VOLT.description: Battery voltage level which is required to arm the aircraft. Set to 0 to allow arming at any voltage.

BATTD_CAPACITY.description: Capacity of the battery in mAh when full

BATTD_CRT_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the critical battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTD_CRT_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a critical battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTD_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT parameter.

BATTD_CURR_MULT.description: Multiplier applied to all current related reports to allow for adjustment if no UAVCAN param access or current splitting applications

BATTD_CURR_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for current monitoring.

BATTD_ESC_INDEX.displayName: ESC Telemetry Index to write to
BATTD_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write voltage, current, consumption and temperature data to. Use 0 to disable.

BATTD_ESC_MASK.description: If 0 all connected ESCs will be used. If non-zero, only those selected in will be used.

BATTD_FL_FLTR.description: Filter frequency in Hertz where a low pass filter is used. This is used to filter out tank slosh from the fuel level reading. A value of -1 disables the filter and unfiltered voltage is used to determine the fuel level. The suggested values at in the range of 0.2 Hz to 0.5 Hz.

BATTD_FL_PIN.description: Analog input pin that fuel level sensor is connected to.Analog Airspeed or RSSI ports can be used for Analog input( some autopilots provide others also). Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

BATTD_FL_V_MULT.description: Voltage multiplier to determine what the full tank voltage reading is. This is calculated as 1 / (Voltage_Full - Voltage_Empty) Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTD_FL_VLT_MIN.description: The voltage seen on the analog pin when the fuel tank is empty. Note: For this type of battery monitor, the voltage seen by the analog pin is displayed as battery voltage on a GCS.

BATTD_FS_CRT_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a critical battery failsafe

BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT.description: What action the vehicle should perform if it hits a low battery failsafe

BATTD_FS_VOLTSRC.description: Voltage type used for detection of low voltage event

BATTD_I2C_ADDR.description: Battery monitor I2C address. If this is zero then probe list of supported addresses

BATTD_LOW_MAH.description: Battery capacity at which the low battery failsafe is triggered. Set to 0 to disable battery remaining failsafe. If the battery capacity drops below this level the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BATTD_LOW_TIMER.description: This is the timeout in seconds before a low voltage event will be triggered. For aircraft with low C batteries it may be necessary to raise this in order to cope with low voltage on long takeoffs. A value of zero disables low voltage errors.

BATTD_LOW_VOLT.description: Battery voltage that triggers a low battery failsafe. Set to 0 to disable. If the battery voltage drops below this voltage continuously for more then the period specified by the BATTD_LOW_TIMER parameter then the vehicle will perform the failsafe specified by the BATTD_FS_LOW_ACT parameter.

BCN_ALT.displayName: Beacon origin's altitude above sealevel in meters
BCN_ALT.description: Beacon origin's altitude above sealevel in meters

BCN_ORIENT_YAW.displayName: Beacon systems rotation from north in degrees
BCN_ORIENT_YAW.description: Beacon systems rotation from north in degrees

BCN_TYPE.displayName: Beacon based position estimation device type
BCN_TYPE.description: What type of beacon based position estimation device is connected

BRD_ALT_CONFIG.description: Select an alternative hardware configuration. A value of zero selects the default configuration for this board. Other values are board specific. Please see the documentation for your board for details on any alternative configuration values that may be available.

BRD_BOOT_DELAY.description: This adds a delay in milliseconds to boot to ensure peripherals initialise fully

BRD_HEAT_LOWMGN.description: Arming check will fail if temp is lower than this margin below BRD_HEAT_TARG. 0 disables the low temperature check

BRD_HEAT_TARG.description: Board heater target temperature for boards with controllable heating units. Set to -1 to disable the heater, please reboot after setting to -1.

BRD_IO_DSHOT.description: This loads the DShot firmware on the IO co-processor

BRD_IO_ENABLE.description: This allows for the IO co-processor on boards with an IOMCU to be disabled. Setting to 2 will enable the IOMCU but not attempt to update firmware on startup

BRD_PWM_VOLT_SEL.description: This sets the voltage max for PWM output pulses. 0 for 3.3V and 1 for 5V output. On boards with an IOMCU that support this parameter this option only affects the 8 main outputs, not the 6 auxiliary outputs. Using 5V output can help to reduce the impact of ESC noise interference corrupting signals to the ESCs.

BRD_RADIO_ABLVL.description: This sets the minimum RSSI of an auto-bind packet for it to be accepted. This should be set so that auto-bind will only happen at short range to minimise the change of an auto-bind happening accidentially

BRD_RADIO_ABTIME.description: When non-zero this sets the time with no transmitter packets before we start looking for auto-bind packets.

BRD_RADIO_BZOFS.description: Set transmitter buzzer note adjustment (adjust frequency up)

BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.displayName: Put radio into FCC test mode
BRD_RADIO_FCCTST.description: If this is enabled then the radio will continuously transmit as required for FCC testing. The transmit channel is set by the value of the parameter. The radio will not work for RC input while this is enabled

BRD_RADIO_PPSCH.description: Channel to show received packet-per-second rate, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_SIGCH.description: Channel to show receive RSSI signal strength, or zero for disabled

BRD_RADIO_STKMD.description: This selects between different stick input modes. The default is mode2, which has throttle on the left stick and pitch on the right stick. You can instead set mode1, which has throttle on the right stick and pitch on the left stick.

BRD_RADIO_TELEM.description: If this is non-zero then telemetry packets will be sent over DSM

BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.displayName: Set radio to factory test channel
BRD_RADIO_TESTCH.description: This sets the radio to a fixed test channel for factory testing. Using a fixed channel avoids the need for binding in factory testing.

BRD_RADIO_TPPSCH.description: Channel to show telemetry packets-per-second value, as received at TX

BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.displayName: RSSI value channel for telemetry data on transmitter
BRD_RADIO_TSIGCH.description: Channel to show telemetry RSSI value as received by TX

BRD_RADIO_TXMAX.description: Set transmitter maximum transmit power (from 1 to 8)

BRD_RADIO_TXPOW.description: Set telemetry transmit power. This is the power level (from 1 to 8) for telemetry packets sent from the RX to the TX

BRD_RADIO_TYPE.displayName: Set type of direct attached radio
BRD_RADIO_TYPE.description: This enables support for direct attached radio receivers
