KDE_NPOLE.description: Sets the number of motor poles to calculate the correct RPM value

LAND_ALT_LOW.description: Altitude during Landing at which vehicle slows to LAND_SPEED

LAND_REPOSITION.description: Enables user input during LAND mode, the landing phase of RTL, and auto mode landings.

LAND_SPEED.description: The descent speed for the final stage of landing in cm/s

LAND_SPEED_HIGH.description: The descent speed for the first stage of landing in cm/s. If this is zero then WPNAV_SPEED_DN is used

LGR_DEPLOY_ALT.description: Altitude where the landing gear will be deployed. This should be lower than the RETRACT_ALT. If zero then altitude is not used for deploying landing gear. Only applies when vehicle is armed.

LGR_DEPLOY_PIN.description: Pin number to use for detection of gear deployment. If set to -1 feedback is disabled. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

LGR_DEPLOY_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the pin should be high when gear are deployed. If set to 0 then then deployed gear level is low.

LGR_OPTIONS.description: Options to retract or deploy landing gear in Auto or Guided mode

LGR_RETRACT_ALT.description: Altitude where the landing gear will be retracted. This should be higher than the DEPLOY_ALT. If zero then altitude is not used for retracting landing gear. Only applies when vehicle is armed.

LGR_WOW_PIN.description: Pin number to use for feedback of weight on wheels condition. If set to -1 feedback is disabled. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

LGR_WOW_POL.displayName: Weight on wheels feedback pin polarity
LGR_WOW_POL.description: Polarity for feedback pin. If this is 1 then the pin should be high when there is weight on wheels. If set to 0 then then weight on wheels level is low.

LOG_BACKEND_TYPE.description: Bitmap of what Logger backend types to enable. Block-based logging is available on SITL and boards with dataflash chips. Multiple backends can be selected.

LOG_BITMASK.description: Bitmap of what on-board log types to enable. This value is made up of the sum of each of the log types you want to be saved. It is usually best just to enable all basiclog types by setting this to 65535. Note that if you want to reduce log sizes you should consider using LOG_FILE_RATEMAX instead of disabling logging items with this parameter.

LOG_BLK_RATEMAX.displayName: Maximum logging rate for block backend
LOG_BLK_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the block backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_DARM_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to any backend when disarmed. A value of zero means that the normal backend rate limit is applied.

LOG_DISARMED.description: If LOG_DISARMED is set to 1 then logging will be enabled at all times including when disarmed. Logging before arming can make for very large logfiles but can help a lot when tracking down startup issues and is necessary if logging of EKF replay data is selected via the LOG_REPLAY parameter. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 2, then logging will be enabled when disarmed, but not if a USB connection is detected. This can be used to prevent unwanted data logs being generated when the vehicle is connected via USB for log downloading or parameter changes. If LOG_DISARMED is set to 3 then logging will happen while disarmed, but if the vehicle never arms then the logs using the filesystem backend will be discarded on the next boot.

values.label: Discard log on reboot if never armed

LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE.displayName: Logging File and Block Backend buffer size max (in kilobytes)
LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE.description: The File and Block backends use a buffer to store data before writing to the block device.  Raising this value may reduce "gaps" in your SD card logging but increases memory usage.  This buffer size may be reduced to free up available memory

LOG_FILE_DSRMROT.displayName: Stop logging to current file on disarm
LOG_FILE_DSRMROT.description: When set, the current log file is closed when the vehicle is disarmed.  If LOG_DISARMED is set then a fresh log will be opened. Applies to the File and Block logging backends.

LOG_FILE_MB_FREE.displayName: Old logs on the SD card will be deleted to maintain this amount of free space
LOG_FILE_MB_FREE.description: Set this such that the free space is larger than your largest typical flight log

LOG_FILE_RATEMAX.displayName: Maximum logging rate for file backend
LOG_FILE_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the file backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_FILE_TIMEOUT.displayName: Timeout before giving up on file writes
LOG_FILE_TIMEOUT.description: This controls the amount of time before failing writes to a log file cause the file to be closed and logging stopped.

LOG_MAV_BUFSIZE.displayName: Maximum AP_Logger MAVLink Backend buffer size
LOG_MAV_BUFSIZE.description: Maximum amount of memory to allocate to AP_Logger-over-mavlink

LOG_MAV_RATEMAX.displayName: Maximum logging rate for mavlink backend
LOG_MAV_RATEMAX.description: This sets the maximum rate that streaming log messages will be logged to the mavlink backend. A value of zero means that rate limiting is disabled.

LOG_MAX_FILES.description: This sets the maximum number of log file that will be written on dataflash or sd card before starting to rotate log number. Limit is capped at 500 logs.

LOG_REPLAY.displayName: Enable logging of information needed for Replay
LOG_REPLAY.description: If LOG_REPLAY is set to 1 then the EKF2 and EKF3 state estimators will log detailed information needed for diagnosing problems with the Kalman filter. LOG_DISARMED must be set to 1 or 2 or else the log will not contain the pre-flight data required for replay testing of the EKF's. It is suggested that you also raise LOG_FILE_BUFSIZE to give more buffer space for logging and use a high quality microSD card to ensure no sensor data is lost.

LOIT_ACC_MAX.description: Loiter maximum correction acceleration in cm/s/s.  Higher values cause the copter to correct position errors more aggressively.

LOIT_ANG_MAX.description: Loiter maximum pilot requested lean angle. Set to zero for 2/3 of PSC_ANGLE_MAX/ANGLE_MAX. The maximum vehicle lean angle is still limited by PSC_ANGLE_MAX/ANGLE_MAX

LOIT_BRK_ACCEL.description: Loiter braking acceleration in cm/s/s. Higher values stop the copter more quickly when the stick is centered.

LOIT_BRK_JERK.description: Loiter braking jerk in cm/s/s/s. Higher values will remove braking faster if the pilot moves the sticks during a braking maneuver.

LOIT_SPEED.description: Defines the maximum speed in cm/s which the aircraft will travel horizontally while in loiter mode

MIS_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask of what options to use in missions.

MIS_RESTART.displayName: Mission Restart when entering Auto mode
MIS_RESTART.description: Controls mission starting point when entering Auto mode (either restart from beginning of mission or resume from last command run)

MIS_TOTAL.description: The number of mission mission items that has been loaded by the ground station. Do not change this manually.

MNT1_DEFLT_MODE.description: Mount default operating mode on startup and after control is returned from autopilot

MNT1_DEVID.description: Mount device ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

MNT1_LEAD_PTCH.description: Servo mount pitch angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current pitch rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT1_LEAD_RLL.description: Servo mount roll angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current roll rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT1_NEUTRAL_X.displayName: Mount roll angle when in neutral position
MNT1_NEUTRAL_X.description: Mount roll angle when in neutral position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_Y.displayName: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position
MNT1_NEUTRAL_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position

MNT1_NEUTRAL_Z.displayName: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position
MNT1_NEUTRAL_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position

MNT1_RC_RATE.description: Pilot rate control's maximum rate.  Set to zero to use angle control

MNT1_RETRACT_X.displayName: Mount roll angle when in retracted position
MNT1_RETRACT_X.description: Mount roll angle when in retracted position

MNT1_RETRACT_Y.displayName: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position
MNT1_RETRACT_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position

MNT1_RETRACT_Z.displayName: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position
MNT1_RETRACT_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position

MNT1_SYSID_DFLT.description: Default Target sysID for the mount to point to

MNT2_DEFLT_MODE.description: Mount default operating mode on startup and after control is returned from autopilot

MNT2_DEVID.description: Mount device ID, taking into account its type, bus and instance

MNT2_LEAD_PTCH.description: Servo mount pitch angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current pitch rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT2_LEAD_RLL.description: Servo mount roll angle output leads the vehicle angle by this amount of time based on current roll rate. Increase until the servo is responsive but does not overshoot

MNT2_NEUTRAL_X.displayName: Mount roll angle when in neutral position
MNT2_NEUTRAL_X.description: Mount roll angle when in neutral position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_Y.displayName: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position
MNT2_NEUTRAL_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in neutral position

MNT2_NEUTRAL_Z.displayName: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position
MNT2_NEUTRAL_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in neutral position

MNT2_RC_RATE.description: Pilot rate control's maximum rate.  Set to zero to use angle control

MNT2_RETRACT_X.displayName: Mount roll angle when in retracted position
MNT2_RETRACT_X.description: Mount roll angle when in retracted position

MNT2_RETRACT_Y.displayName: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position
MNT2_RETRACT_Y.description: Mount pitch angle when in retracted position

MNT2_RETRACT_Z.displayName: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position
MNT2_RETRACT_Z.description: Mount yaw angle when in retracted position

MNT2_SYSID_DFLT.description: Default Target sysID for the mount to point to

MOT_BAT_CURR_MAX.description: Maximum current over which maximum throttle is limited (0 = Disabled)

MOT_BAT_CURR_TC.description: Time constant used to limit the maximum current

MOT_BAT_IDX.description: Which battery monitor should be used for doing compensation

MOT_BAT_VOLT_MAX.description: Battery voltage compensation maximum voltage (voltage above this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 4.2 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

MOT_BAT_VOLT_MIN.description: Battery voltage compensation minimum voltage (voltage below this will have no additional scaling effect on thrust).  Recommend 3.3 * cell count, 0 = Disabled

MOT_BOOST_SCALE.description: Booster motor output scaling factor vs main throttle.  The output to the BoostThrottle servo will be the main throttle times this scaling factor. A higher scaling factor will put more of the load on the booster motor. A value of 1 will set the BoostThrottle equal to the main throttle.

MOT_HOVER_LEARN.description: Enable/Disable automatic learning of hover throttle

MOT_PWM_MAX.description: This sets the max PWM value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

MOT_PWM_MIN.description: This sets the min PWM output value in microseconds that will ever be output to the motors

MOT_PWM_TYPE.description: This selects the output PWM type, allowing for normal PWM continuous output, OneShot, brushed or DShot motor output.PWMRange and PWMAngle are PWM special/rare cases for ESCs that dont calibrate normally (some Sub motors) or where each ESC must have its PWM range set individually using the Servo params instead of PWM_MIN/MAX parameters.

MOT_SAFE_DISARM.displayName: Motor PWM output disabled when disarmed
MOT_SAFE_DISARM.description: Disables motor PWM output when disarmed

MOT_SAFE_TIME.displayName: Time taken to disable and enable the motor PWM output when disarmed and armed.
MOT_SAFE_TIME.description: Time taken to disable and enable the motor PWM output when disarmed and armed.

MOT_SLEW_DN_TIME.displayName: Output slew time for decreasing throttle
MOT_SLEW_DN_TIME.description: Time in seconds to slew output from full to zero. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change.  Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

MOT_SLEW_UP_TIME.displayName: Output slew time for increasing throttle
MOT_SLEW_UP_TIME.description: Time in seconds to slew output from zero to full. This is used to limit the rate at which output can change. Range is constrained between 0 and 0.5.

MOT_SPIN_ARM.description: Point at which the motors start to spin expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be lower than MOT_SPIN_MIN.

MOT_SPIN_MAX.description: Point at which the thrust saturates expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range

MOT_SPIN_MIN.description: Point at which the thrust starts expressed as a number from 0 to 1 in the entire output range.  Should be higher than MOT_SPIN_ARM.

MOT_SPOOL_TIM_DN.description: Time taken to spool down the motors from min to zero throttle. If set to 0 then SPOOL_TIME is used instead.

MOT_SPOOL_TIME.description: Time in seconds to spool up the motors from zero to min throttle.

MOT_THST_EXPO.description: Motor thrust curve exponent (0.0 for linear to 1.0 for second order curve)

MOT_THST_HOVER.description: Motor thrust needed to hover expressed as a number from 0 to 1

MOT_YAW_HEADROOM.description: Yaw control is given at least this pwm in microseconds range

MOT_YAW_SV_ANGLE.description: Yaw servo's maximum lean angle (Tricopter only)

MSP_OPTIONS.description: A bitmask to set some MSP specific options: EnableTelemetryMode-allows "push" mode telemetry when only rx line of OSD ic connected to autopilot,  EnableBTFLFonts-uses indexes corresponding to Betaflight fonts if OSD uses those instead of ArduPilot fonts. EnableINAVFonts uses INAV fonts and overrides EnableBTFLFonts if that option is enabled.

MSP_OSD_NCELLS.description: Used for average cell voltage calculation

NET_NETMASK.description: Allows setting static subnet mask. The value is a count of consecutive bits. Examples: 24 = 255.255.255.0, 16 = 255.255.0.0

NET_P{idx}_TYPE.description: Port type for network serial port. For the two client types a valid destination IP address must be set. For the two server types either 0.0.0.0 or a local address can be used. The UDP client type will use broadcast if the IP is set to 255.255.255.255 and will use UDP multicast if the IP is in the multicast address range.

NMEA_MSG_EN.description: This is a bitmask of enabled NMEA messages. All messages will be sent consecutively at the same rate interval

NMEA_RATE_MS.description: NMEA Output rate. This controls the interval at which all the enabled NMEA messages are sent. Most NMEA systems expect 100ms (10Hz) or slower.

NTF_BUZZ_ON_LVL.description: Specifies pin level that indicates buzzer should play

NTF_BUZZ_PIN.description: Enables to connect active buzzer to arbitrary pin. Requires 3-pin buzzer or additional MOSFET! Some the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

NTF_BUZZ_TYPES.description: Controls what types of Buzzer will be enabled

NTF_BUZZ_VOLUME.description: Control the volume of the buzzer

NTF_DISPLAY_TYPE.description: This sets up the type of on-board I2C display. Disabled by default.

NTF_LED_BRIGHT.description: Select the RGB LED brightness level. When USB is connected brightness will never be higher than low regardless of the setting.

NTF_LED_LEN.description: The number of Serial LED's to use for notifications (NeoPixel's and ProfiLED)

NTF_LED_OVERRIDE.displayName: Specifies colour source for the RGBLed
NTF_LED_OVERRIDE.description: Specifies the source for the colours and brightness for the LED.  OutbackChallenge conforms to the MedicalExpress (https://uavchallenge.org/medical-express/) rules, essentially "Green" is disarmed (safe-to-approach), "Red" is armed (not safe-to-approach). Traffic light is a simplified color set, red when armed, yellow when the safety switch is not surpressing outputs (but disarmed), and green when outputs are surpressed and disarmed, the LED will blink faster if disarmed and failing arming checks.

NTF_LED_TYPES.description: Controls what types of LEDs will be enabled

NTF_OREO_THEME.description: Enable/Disable Solo Oreo LED driver, 0 to disable, 1 for Aircraft theme, 2 for Rover theme

OA_BR_CONT_ANGLE.displayName: BendyRuler's bearing change resistance threshold angle
OA_BR_CONT_ANGLE.description: BendyRuler will resist changing current bearing if the change in bearing is over this angle

OA_BR_CONT_RATIO.displayName: Obstacle Avoidance margin ratio for BendyRuler to change bearing significantly
OA_BR_CONT_RATIO.description: BendyRuler will avoid changing bearing unless ratio of previous margin from obstacle (or fence) to present calculated margin is atleast this much.

OA_BR_LOOKAHEAD.displayName: Object Avoidance look ahead distance maximum
OA_BR_LOOKAHEAD.description: Object Avoidance will look this many meters ahead of vehicle

OA_BR_TYPE.description: BendyRuler will search for clear path along the direction defined by this parameter

OA_DB_ALT_MIN.displayName: OADatabase minimum altitude above home before storing obstacles
OA_DB_ALT_MIN.description: OADatabase will reject obstacles if vehicle's altitude above home is below this parameter, in a 3 meter radius around home. Set 0 to disable this feature.

OA_DB_BEAM_WIDTH.description: Beam width of incoming lidar data

OA_DB_DIST_MAX.description: Maximum distance of objects held in database.  Set to zero to disable the limits

OA_DB_EXPIRE.description: OADatabase item timeout. The time an item will linger without any updates before it expires. Zero means never expires which is useful for a sent-once static environment but terrible for dynamic ones.

OA_DB_OUTPUT.description: OADatabase output level to configure which database objects are sent to the ground station. All data is always available internally for avoidance algorithms.

values.label: Send HIGH and NORMAL importance items

OA_DB_QUEUE_SIZE.displayName: OADatabase queue maximum number of points
OA_DB_QUEUE_SIZE.description: OADatabase queue maximum number of points. This in an input buffer size. Larger means it can handle larger bursts of incoming data points to filter into the database. No impact on cpu, only RAM. Recommend larger for faster datalinks or for sensors that generate a lot of data.

OA_DB_RADIUS_MIN.description: Minimum radius of objects held in database

OA_DB_SIZE.description: OADatabase maximum number of points. Set to 0 to disable the OA Database. Larger means more points but is more cpu intensive to process

OA_MARGIN_MAX.description: Object Avoidance will ignore objects more than this many meters from vehicle

OA_OPTIONS.displayName: Options while recovering from Object Avoidance
OA_OPTIONS.description: Bitmask which will govern vehicles behaviour while recovering from Obstacle Avoidance (i.e Avoidance is turned off after the path ahead is clear).

OA_TYPE.displayName: Object Avoidance Path Planning algorithm to use

OSD_ARM_SCR.description: Screen to be shown on Arm event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_BTN_DELAY.description: Debounce time in ms for stick commanded parameter navigation.

OSD_CELL_COUNT.description: Used for average cell voltage display. -1 disables, 0 uses cell count autodetection for well charged LIPO/LIION batteries at connection, other values manually select cell count used.

OSD_CHAN.description: This sets the channel used to switch different OSD screens.

OSD_DSARM_SCR.description: Screen to be shown on disarm event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_FONT.description: This sets which OSD font to use. It is an integer from 0 to the number of fonts available

OSD_FS_SCR.description: Screen to be shown on failsafe event. Zero to disable the feature.

OSD_H_OFFSET.description: Sets horizontal offset of the osd inside image

OSD_OPTIONS.description: This sets options that change the display

OSD_SB_H_OFS.description: Extends the spacing between the sidebar elements by this amount of columns. Positive values increases the width to the right of the screen.

OSD_SB_V_EXT.description: Increase of vertical length of the sidebar itens by this amount of lines. Applied equally both above and below the default setting.

OSD_SW_METHOD.description: This sets the method used to switch different OSD screens.

values.label: switch to next screen if channel value was changed
select screen based on pwm ranges specified for each screen
switch to next screen after low to high transition and every 1s while channel value is high

OSD_TYPE.description: OSD type. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

OSD_TYPE2.description: OSD type 2. TXONLY makes the OSD parameter selection available to other modules even if there is no native OSD support on the board, for instance CRSF.

OSD_UNITS.description: Sets the units to use in displaying items

OSD_V_OFFSET.description: Sets vertical offset of the osd inside image

OSD_W_ACRVOLT.displayName: Avg Cell Resting Volt warn level
OSD_W_ACRVOLT.description: Set level at which ACRVOLT item will flash

OSD_W_AVGCELLV.description: Set level at which AVGCELLV item will flash

OSD_W_BATVOLT.description: Set level at which BAT_VOLT item will flash

OSD_W_LQ.description: Set level at which RC_LQ item will flash (%)

OSD_W_NSAT.description: Set level at which NSAT item will flash

OSD_W_RESTVOLT.description: Set level at which RESTVOLT item will flash

OSD_W_RSSI.description: Set level at which RSSI item will flash (in positive % or negative dBm values as applicable). 30% or -100dBm are defaults.

OSD_W_SNR.description: Set level at which RC_SNR item will flash (in db)

OSD_W_TERR.description: Set level below which TER_HGT item will flash. -1 disables.

OSD{idx}_ACRVOLT_EN.description: Displays resting voltage for the average cell. WARNING: this can be inaccurate if the cell count is not detected or set properly. If the  the battery is far from fully charged the detected cell count might not be accurate if auto cell count detection is used (OSD_CELL_COUNT=0).

OSD{idx}_ASPD1_EN.description: Displays airspeed reported directly from primary airspeed sensor

OSD{idx}_ASPD2_EN.description: Displays airspeed reported directly from secondary airspeed sensor

OSD{idx}_ASPEED_EN.description: Displays airspeed value being used by TECS (fused value)

OSD{idx}_ATEMP_EN.description: Displays temperature reported by primary airspeed sensor

OSD{idx}_AVGCELLV_EN.description: Displays average cell voltage. WARNING: this can be inaccurate if the cell count is not detected or set properly. If the  the battery is far from fully charged the detected cell count might not be accurate if auto cell count detection is used (OSD_CELL_COUNT=0).

OSD{idx}_BATTBAR_EN.description: Displays battery usage bar (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_BTEMP_EN.description: Displays temperature reported by secondary barometer

OSD{idx}_CALLSIGN_EN.description: Displays callsign from callsign.txt on microSD card

OSD{idx}_CELLVOLT_EN.description: Displays average cell voltage (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_CHAN_MAX.description: This sets the PWM upper limit for this screen

OSD{idx}_CHAN_MIN.description: This sets the PWM lower limit for this screen

OSD{idx}_CLK_EN.description: Displays a clock panel based on AP_RTC local time

OSD{idx}_CRSSHAIR_EN.description: Displays artificial horizon crosshair (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_CRSSHAIR_X.description: Horizontal position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_CRSSHAIR_Y.description: Vertical position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_ESC_IDX.description: Index of the ESC to use for displaying ESC information. 0 means use the ESC with the highest value.

OSD{idx}_ESCAMPS_EN.description: Displays the current of the ESC with the highest rpm of all active ESCs, or of a specific ESC if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD{idx}_ESCRPM_EN.description: Displays highest rpm of all active ESCs, or of a specific ESC if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD{idx}_ESCTEMP_EN.description: Displays highest temp of all active ESCs, or of a specific ECS if OSDx_ESC_IDX is set

OSD{idx}_FENCE_EN.description: Displays indication of fence enable and breach

OSD{idx}_FONT.displayName: Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)
OSD{idx}_FONT.description: Sets the font index for this screen (MSP DisplayPort only)

OSD{idx}_HOME_EN.description: Displays distance and relative direction to HOME

OSD{idx}_HOMEDIR_EN.description: Displays relative direction to HOME (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_HOMEDIST_EN.description: Displays distance from HOME (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_HOMEDIST_X.description: Horizontal position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_HOMEDIST_Y.description: Vertical position on screen (MSP OSD only)

OSD{idx}_PITCH_EN.description: Displays degrees of pitch from level

OSD{idx}_RC_ANT_EN.description: Displays the current RC link active antenna

OSD{idx}_RC_LQ_EN.description: Displays the RC link quality (uplink, 0 to 100%) and also RF mode if bit 7 of OSD_OPTIONS is set

OSD{idx}_RC_PWR_EN.description: Displays the RC link transmit (TX) power in mW or W, depending on level

OSD{idx}_RC_SNR_EN.description: Displays RC link signal to noise ratio in dB

OSD{idx}_RNGF_EN.description: Displays a rangefinder's distance in cm

OSD{idx}_ROLL_EN.description: Displays degrees of roll from level

OSD{idx}_RSSIDBM_EN.description: Displays RC link signal strength in dBm

OSD{idx}_TEMP_EN.description: Displays temperature reported by primary barometer

OSD{idx}_THROTTLE_EN.description: Displays actual throttle percentage being sent to motor(s)

OSD{idx}_TXT_RES.displayName: Sets the overlay text resolution (MSP DisplayPort only)
OSD{idx}_TXT_RES.description: Sets the overlay text resolution for this screen to either SD 30x16 or HD 50x18/60x22 (MSP DisplayPort only)

OSD{idx}_WAYPOINT_EN.description: Displays bearing and distance to next waypoint

OSD{idx}_WIND_EN.description: Displays wind speed and relative direction, on Rover this is the apparent wind speed and direction from the windvane, if fitted

OSD6_SAVE_X.description: Horizontal position of Save button on screen

OSD6_SAVE_Y.description: Vertical position of Save button on screen

PHLD_BRAKE_ANGLE.description: PosHold flight mode's max lean angle during braking in centi-degrees

PHLD_BRAKE_RATE.description: PosHold flight mode's rotation rate during braking in deg/sec

PILOT_ACCEL_Z.description: The vertical acceleration used when pilot is controlling the altitude

PILOT_SPEED_DN.description: The maximum vertical descending velocity the pilot may request in cm/s.  If 0 PILOT_SPEED_UP value is used.

PILOT_SPEED_UP.description: The maximum vertical ascending velocity the pilot may request in cm/s

PILOT_THR_BHV.description: Bitmask containing various throttle stick options. TX with sprung throttle can set PILOT_THR_BHV to "1" so motor feedback when landed starts from mid-stick instead of bottom of stick.

PILOT_THR_FILT.description: Throttle filter cutoff (Hz) - active whenever altitude control is inactive - 0 to disable

PILOT_TKOFF_ALT.description: Altitude that altitude control modes will climb to when a takeoff is triggered with the throttle stick.

PILOT_Y_EXPO.description: Pilot controlled yaw expo to allow faster rotation when stick at edges

PILOT_Y_RATE.description: Pilot controlled yaw rate max.  Used in all pilot controlled modes except Acro

PILOT_Y_RATE_TC.displayName: Pilot yaw rate control input time constant
PILOT_Y_RATE_TC.description: Pilot yaw rate control input time constant.  Low numbers lead to sharper response, higher numbers to softer response

PLDP_DELAY.description: Delay after release, in seconds, before aircraft starts to climb back to starting altitude.

PLDP_RNG_MAX.displayName: Payload Place maximum range finder altitude
PLDP_RNG_MAX.description: Maximum range finder altitude in m to trigger payload touchdown, set to zero to disable.

PLDP_SPEED_DN.description: The maximum vertical decent velocity in m/s. If 0 LAND_SPEED value is used.

PLDP_THRESH.description: Ratio of vertical thrust during decent below which payload touchdown will trigger.

PLND_ACC_P_NSE.description: Kalman Filter Accelerometer Noise, higher values weight the input from the camera more, accels less

PLND_ALT_CUTOFF.description: The altitude (rangefinder distance) below which we stop using the precision landing sensor and continue landing

PLND_ALT_MAX.description: Vehicle will continue landing vertically until this height if target is not found. Below this height if landing target is not found, landing retry/failsafe might be attempted. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

PLND_ALT_MIN.description: Vehicle will continue landing vertically even if target is lost below this height. This needs a rangefinder to work. Set to zero to disable this.

PLND_BUS.description: Precland sensor bus for I2C sensors.

PLND_CAM_POS_X.description: X position of the camera in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin.

PLND_CAM_POS_Y.description: Y position of the camera in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin.

PLND_CAM_POS_Z.description: Z position of the camera in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin.

PLND_EST_TYPE.description: Specifies the estimation method to be used

PLND_LAG.description: Precision Landing sensor lag, to cope with variable landing_target latency

PLND_LAND_OFS_X.description: Desired landing position of the camera forward of the target in vehicle body frame

PLND_LAND_OFS_Y.description: desired landing position of the camera right of the target in vehicle body frame

PLND_RET_BEHAVE.description: Prec Land will do the action selected by this parameter if a retry to a landing is needed

values.label: Go to the last location where landing target was detected
Go towards the approximate location of the detected landing target

PLND_RET_MAX.displayName: PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landing
PLND_RET_MAX.description: PrecLand Maximum number of retires for a failed landing. Set to zero to disable landing retry.

PLND_STRICT.description: How strictly should the vehicle land on the target if target is lost

PLND_TIMEOUT.description: Time for which vehicle continues descend even if target is lost. After this time period, vehicle will attempt a landing retry depending on PLND_STRICT parameter.

PLND_XY_DIST_MAX.displayName: Precision Landing maximum distance to target before descending
PLND_XY_DIST_MAX.description: The vehicle will not start descending if the landing target is detected and it is further than this many meters away. Set 0 to always descend.

PLND_YAW_ALIGN.description: Yaw angle from body x-axis to sensor x-axis.

POI_DIST_MAX.description: POI's max distance (in meters) from the vehicle

PREV_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to used to trigger parameter reversion

PRX_ALT_MIN.description: Minimum altitude below which proximity should not work.

PRX_FILT.description: Cutoff frequency for low pass filter applied to each face in the proximity boundary

PRX_IGN_GND.description: Ignore proximity data that is within 1 meter of the ground below the vehicle. This requires a downward facing rangefinder

PRX_LOG_RAW.description: Set this parameter to one if logging unfiltered(raw) distances from sensor should be enabled

PRX{idx}_ADDR.description: The bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

PRX{idx}_MAX.description: Maximum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX{idx}_MIN.description: Minimum expected range for Proximity Sensor. Setting this to 0 will set value to manufacturer reported range.

PRX{idx}_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

PRX{idx}_TYPE.description: What type of proximity sensor is connected

PSC_ACC_XY_FILT.description: Lower values will slow the response of the navigation controller and reduce twitchiness

PSC_ACCZ_D.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller D gain.  Compensates for short-term change in desired vertical acceleration vs actual acceleration

PSC_ACCZ_D_FF.description: FF D Gain which produces an output that is proportional to the rate of change of the target

PSC_ACCZ_FLTD.displayName: Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in Hz
PSC_ACCZ_FLTD.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller derivative frequency in Hz

PSC_ACCZ_FLTE.displayName: Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in Hz
PSC_ACCZ_FLTE.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller error frequency in Hz

PSC_ACCZ_FLTT.displayName: Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in Hz
PSC_ACCZ_FLTT.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller target frequency in Hz

PSC_ACCZ_I.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration

PSC_ACCZ_IMAX.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the maximum pwm that the I term will generate

PSC_ACCZ_P.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller P gain.  Converts the difference between desired vertical acceleration and actual acceleration into a motor output

PSC_ACCZ_PDMX.description: Acceleration (vertical) controller PD sum maximum.  The maximum/minimum value that the sum of the P and D term can output

PSC_ACCZ_SMAX.description: Sets an upper limit on the slew rate produced by the combined P and D gains. If the amplitude of the control action produced by the rate feedback exceeds this value, then the D+P gain is reduced to respect the limit. This limits the amplitude of high frequency oscillations caused by an excessive gain. The limit should be set to no more than 25% of the actuators maximum slew rate to allow for load effects. Note: The gain will not be reduced to less than 10% of the nominal value. A value of zero will disable this feature.

PSC_ANGLE_MAX.description: Maximum lean angle autopilot can request.  Set to zero to use ANGLE_MAX parameter value

PSC_JERK_XY.displayName: Jerk limit for the horizontal kinematic input shaping
PSC_JERK_XY.description: Jerk limit of the horizontal kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

PSC_JERK_Z.displayName: Jerk limit for the vertical kinematic input shaping
PSC_JERK_Z.description: Jerk limit of the vertical kinematic path generation used to determine how quickly the aircraft varies the acceleration target

PSC_POSXY_P.description: Position controller P gain.  Converts the distance (in the latitude direction) to the target location into a desired speed which is then passed to the loiter latitude rate controller

PSC_POSZ_P.description: Position (vertical) controller P gain.  Converts the difference between the desired altitude and actual altitude into a climb or descent rate which is passed to the throttle rate controller

PSC_VELXY_D.description: Velocity (horizontal) D gain.  Corrects short-term changes in velocity

PSC_VELXY_FF.description: Velocity (horizontal) feed forward gain.  Converts the difference between desired velocity to a target acceleration

PSC_VELXY_FLTD.description: Velocity (horizontal) input filter.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D term

PSC_VELXY_FLTE.description: Velocity (horizontal) input filter.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms

PSC_VELXY_I.description: Velocity (horizontal) I gain.  Corrects long-term difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration

PSC_VELXY_IMAX.description: Velocity (horizontal) integrator maximum.  Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output

PSC_VELXY_P.description: Velocity (horizontal) P gain.  Converts the difference between desired and actual velocity to a target acceleration

PSC_VELZ_D.description: Velocity (vertical) controller D gain.  Corrects short-term changes in velocity

PSC_VELZ_FF.description: Velocity (vertical) controller Feed Forward gain.  Produces an output that is proportional to the magnitude of the target

PSC_VELZ_FLTD.displayName: Velocity (vertical) input filter for D term
PSC_VELZ_FLTD.description: Velocity (vertical) input filter for D term.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for D terms

PSC_VELZ_FLTE.description: Velocity (vertical) error filter.  This filter (in Hz) is applied to the input for P and I terms

PSC_VELZ_I.description: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain.  Corrects long-term difference in desired velocity to a target acceleration

PSC_VELZ_IMAX.description: Velocity (vertical) controller I gain maximum.  Constrains the target acceleration that the I gain will output

PSC_VELZ_P.description: Velocity (vertical) controller P gain.  Converts the difference between desired vertical speed and actual speed into a desired acceleration that is passed to the throttle acceleration controller

QUIK_ANGLE_MAX.displayName: maximum angle error for tune abort
QUIK_ANGLE_MAX.description: If while tuning the angle error goes over this limit then the tune will aborts to prevent a bad oscillation in the case of the tuning algorithm failing. If you get an error "Tuning: attitude error ABORTING" and you think it is a false positive then you can either raise this parameter or you can try increasing the QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME to do the tune more slowly. A value of zero disables this check.

QUIK_AUTO_FILTER.description: When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

QUIK_AUTO_SAVE.description: Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune

QUIK_DOUBLE_TIME.description: Time to double a tuning parameter. Raise this for a slower tune.

QUIK_GAIN_MARGIN.description: Reduction in gain after oscillation detected. Raise this number to get a more conservative tune

QUIK_MAX_REDUCE.description: This controls how much quicktune is allowed to lower gains from the original gains. If the vehicle already has a reasonable tune and is not oscillating then you can set this to zero to prevent gain reductions. The default of 20% is reasonable for most vehicles. Using a maximum gain reduction lowers the chance of an angle P oscillation happening if quicktune gets a false positive oscillation at a low gain, which can result in very low rate gains and a dangerous angle P oscillation.

QUIK_OPTIONS.description: Additional options. When the Two Position Switch option is enabled then a high switch position will start the tune, low will disable the tune. you should also set a QUIK_AUTO_SAVE time so that you will be able to save the tune.

QUIK_OSC_SMAX.description: Threshold for oscillation detection. A lower value will lead to a more conservative tune.

QUIK_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to use to control tuning stop/start/save

QUIK_RP_PI_RATIO.description: Ratio between P and I gains for roll and pitch. Raise this to get a lower I gain

QUIK_Y_PI_RATIO.description: Ratio between P and I gains for yaw. Raise this to get a lower I gain

QUIK_YAW_D_MAX.description: Maximum value for yaw D gain

QUIK_YAW_P_MAX.description: Maximum value for yaw P gain

RALLY_INCL_HOME.description: Controls if Home is included as a Rally point (i.e. as a safe landing place) for RTL

RALLY_LIMIT_KM.description: Maximum distance to rally point. If the closest rally point is more than this number of kilometers from the current position and the home location is closer than any of the rally points from the current position then do RTL to home rather than to the closest rally point. This prevents a leftover rally point from a different airfield being used accidentally. If this is set to 0 then the closest rally point is always used.

RALLY_TOTAL.description: Number of rally points currently loaded

RC_FS_TIMEOUT.description: RC failsafe will trigger this many seconds after loss of RC

RC_OVERRIDE_TIME.description: Timeout after which RC overrides will no longer be used, and RC input will resume, 0 will disable RC overrides, -1 will never timeout, and continue using overrides until they are disabled

RC_PROTOCOLS.description: Bitmask of enabled RC protocols. Allows narrowing the protocol detection to only specific types of RC receivers which can avoid issues with incorrect detection. Set to 1 to enable all protocols.

RC_SPEED.description: This is the speed in Hertz that your ESCs will receive updates

RC{idx}_DZ.description: PWM dead zone in microseconds around trim or bottom

RC{idx}_MAX.description: RC maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC{idx}_MIN.description: RC minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RC{idx}_OPTION.description: Function assigned to this RC channel

values.label: Test autotuned gains after tune is complete

RC{idx}_REVERSED.description: Reverse channel input. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this input channel.

RC{idx}_TRIM.description: RC trim (neutral) PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

RCK_DEBUG.description: Sends Rockblock debug text to GCS via statustexts

RCK_ENABLE.description: Enables the Rockblock sending and recieving

RCK_FORCEHL.description: Automatically enables High Latency mode if not already enabled

RCK_PERIOD.description: When in High Latency mode, send Rockblock updates every N seconds

RCMAP_PITCH.description: Pitch channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Pitch is normally on channel 2, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_ROLL.description: Roll channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Roll is normally on channel 1, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_THROTTLE.description: Throttle channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Throttle is normally on channel 3, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter. Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RCMAP_YAW.description: Yaw channel number. This is useful when you have a RC transmitter that can't change the channel order easily. Yaw (also known as rudder) is normally on channel 4, but you can move it to any channel with this parameter.  Reboot is required for changes to take effect.

RELAY{idx}_DEFAULT.description: Should the relay default to on or off, this only applies to RELAYx_FUNC "Relay" (1). All other uses will pick the appropriate default output state from within the controlling function's parameters. Note that if INVERTED is set then the default is inverted.

RELAY{idx}_FUNCTION.description: The function the relay channel is mapped to.

RELAY{idx}_INVERTED.description: Should the relay output signal be inverted. If enabled, relay on would be pin low and relay off would be pin high. NOTE: this impact's DEFAULT.

RELAY{idx}_PIN.description: Digital pin number for relay control. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND_FILT.description: Rangefinder filter to smooth distance.  Set to zero to disable filtering

RNGFND{idx}_ADDR.description: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RNGFND{idx}_FUNCTION.description: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFND{idx}_GNDCLEAR.displayName: Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the ground
RNGFND{idx}_GNDCLEAR.description: This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFND{idx}_MAX_CM.description: Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND{idx}_MIN_CM.description: Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFND{idx}_OFFSET.description: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFND{idx}_PIN.description: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFND{idx}_POS_X.description: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND{idx}_POS_Y.description: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND{idx}_POS_Z.description: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFND{idx}_PWRRNG.description: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFND{idx}_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFND{idx}_RMETRIC.description: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFND{idx}_SCALING.description: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFND{idx}_SNR_MIN.description: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFND{idx}_STOP_PIN.description: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFND{idx}_WSP_AVG.description: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFND{idx}_WSP_FRQ.description: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFND{idx}_WSP_MAVG.description: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFND{idx}_WSP_MEDF.description: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFND{idx}_WSP_THR.description: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments

RNGFNDA_ADDR.description: This sets the bus address of the sensor, where applicable. Used for the I2C and DroneCAN sensors to allow for multiple sensors on different addresses.

RNGFNDA_FUNCTION.description: Control over what function is used to calculate distance. For a linear function, the distance is (voltage-offset)*scaling. For a inverted function the distance is (offset-voltage)*scaling. For a hyperbolic function the distance is scaling/(voltage-offset). The functions return the distance in meters.

RNGFNDA_GNDCLEAR.displayName: Distance (in cm) from the range finder to the ground
RNGFNDA_GNDCLEAR.description: This parameter sets the expected range measurement(in cm) that the range finder should return when the vehicle is on the ground.

RNGFNDA_MAX_CM.description: Maximum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFNDA_MIN_CM.description: Minimum distance in centimeters that rangefinder can reliably read

RNGFNDA_OFFSET.description: Offset in volts for zero distance for analog rangefinders. Offset added to distance in centimeters for PWM lidars

RNGFNDA_PIN.description: Analog or PWM input pin that rangefinder is connected to. Analog RSSI or Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RNGFNDA_POS_X.description: X position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFNDA_POS_Y.description: Y position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFNDA_POS_Z.description: Z position of the rangefinder in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin. Use the zero range datum point if supplied.

RNGFNDA_PWRRNG.description: This parameter sets the estimated terrain distance in meters above which the sensor will be put into a power saving mode (if available). A value of zero means power saving is not enabled

RNGFNDA_RECV_ID.description: The receive ID of the CAN frames. A value of zero means all IDs are accepted.

RNGFNDA_RMETRIC.description: This parameter sets whether an analog rangefinder is ratiometric. Most analog rangefinders are ratiometric, meaning that their output voltage is influenced by the supply voltage. Some analog rangefinders (such as the SF/02) have their own internal voltage regulators so they are not ratiometric.

RNGFNDA_SCALING.description: Scaling factor between rangefinder reading and distance. For the linear and inverted functions this is in meters per volt. For the hyperbolic function the units are meterVolts. For Maxbotix serial sonar this is unit conversion to meters.

RNGFNDA_SNR_MIN.description: RangeFinder Minimum signal strength (SNR) to accept distance

RNGFNDA_STOP_PIN.description: Digital pin that enables/disables rangefinder measurement for the pwm rangefinder. A value of -1 means no pin. If this is set, then the pin is set to 1 to enable the rangefinder and set to 0 to disable it. This is used to enable powersaving when out of range. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RNGFNDA_WSP_AVG.description: Sets the number of pulses to be used in multi-pulse averaging mode. In this mode, a sequence of rapid fire ranges are taken and then averaged to improve the accuracy of the measurement

RNGFNDA_WSP_FRQ.description: Sets the repetition frequency of the ranging operation in Hertz. Upon entering the desired frequency the system will calculate the nearest frequency that it can handle according to the resolution of internal timers.

RNGFNDA_WSP_MAVG.description: Sets the number of historic range results to use for calculating the current range result. When MAVG is greater than 1, the current range result will be the current measured value averaged with the N-1 previous results

RNGFNDA_WSP_MEDF.description: Sets the window size for the real-time median filter. When MEDF is greater than 0 the median filter is active

RNGFNDA_WSP_THR.description: Sets the system sensitivity. Larger values of THR represent higher sensitivity. The system may limit the maximum value of THR to prevent excessive false alarm rates based on settings made at the factory. Set to -1 for automatic threshold adjustments

RPM{idx}_DC_ID.description: DroneCAN sensor ID to assign to this backend

RPM{idx}_ESC_INDEX.displayName: ESC Telemetry Index to write RPM to
RPM{idx}_ESC_INDEX.description: ESC Telemetry Index to write RPM to. Use 0 to disable.

RPM{idx}_ESC_MASK.displayName: Bitmask of ESC telemetry channels to average
RPM{idx}_ESC_MASK.description: Mask of channels which support ESC rpm telemetry. RPM telemetry of the selected channels will be averaged

RPM{idx}_MAX.description: Maximum RPM to report. Only used on type = GPIO.

RPM{idx}_MIN.description: Minimum RPM to report. Only used on type = GPIO.

RPM{idx}_MIN_QUAL.description: Minimum data quality to be used

RPM{idx}_PIN.description: Which digital GPIO pin to use. Only used on type = GPIO. Some common values are given, but see the Wiki's "GPIOs" page for how to determine the pin number for a given autopilot.

RPM{idx}_SCALING.description: Scaling factor between sensor reading and RPM.

RPM{idx}_TYPE.description: What type of RPM sensor is connected

RSSI_ANA_PIN.description: Pin used to read the RSSI voltage or PWM value. Analog Airspeed ports can be used for Analog inputs (some autopilots provide others also), Non-IOMCU Servo/MotorOutputs can be used for PWM input when configured as "GPIOs". Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins" for analog pin or "GPIOs", if PWM input type, to determine pin number.

RSSI_CHAN_HIGH.description: PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_CHAN_LOW

RSSI_CHAN_LOW.description: PWM value that the radio receiver will put on the RSSI_CHANNEL or RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers output inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_CHAN_HIGH

RSSI_CHANNEL.description: The channel number where RSSI will be output by the radio receiver (5 and above).

RSSI_PIN_HIGH.description: RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the strongest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be lower than RSSI_PIN_LOW. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

RSSI_PIN_LOW.description: RSSI pin's voltage received on the RSSI_ANA_PIN when the signal strength is the weakest. Some radio receivers put out inverted values so this value may be higher than RSSI_PIN_HIGH. When using pin 103, the maximum value of the parameter is 3.3V.

RSSI_TYPE.description: Radio Receiver RSSI type. If your radio receiver supports RSSI of some kind, set it here, then set its associated RSSI_XXXXX parameters, if any.

RTL_ALT.description: The minimum alt above home the vehicle will climb to before returning.  If the vehicle is flying higher than this value it will return at its current altitude.

RTL_ALT_FINAL.description: This is the altitude the vehicle will move to as the final stage of Returning to Launch or after completing a mission.  Set to zero to land.

RTL_ALT_TYPE.description: RTL altitude type.  Set to 1 for Terrain following during RTL and then set WPNAV_RFND_USE=1 to use rangefinder or WPNAV_RFND_USE=0 to use Terrain database

RTL_CLIMB_MIN.description: The vehicle will climb this many cm during the initial climb portion of the RTL

RTL_CONE_SLOPE.description: Defines a cone above home which determines maximum climb

RTL_LOIT_TIME.description: Time (in milliseconds) to loiter above home before beginning final descent

RTL_OPTIONS.description: Options that can be applied to change RTL mode behaviour

RTL_SPEED.description: Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain horizontally while flying home. If this is set to zero, WPNAV_SPEED will be used instead.

RTUN_AUTO_FILTER.description: When enabled the PID filter settings are automatically set based on INS_GYRO_FILTER

RTUN_AUTO_SAVE.description: Number of seconds after completion of tune to auto-save. This is useful when using a 2 position switch for quicktune

RTUN_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to use to control tuning stop/start/save

RTUN_SPD_FFRATIO.description: Ratio between measured response and CRUISE_THROTTLE value. Raise this to get a higher CRUISE_THROTTLE value

RTUN_SPD_I_RATIO.displayName: Rover Quicktune Speed FF to I ratio
RTUN_SPD_I_RATIO.description: Ratio between speed FF and I gain. Raise this to get a higher I gain, 0 to leave I unchanged

RTUN_SPD_P_RATIO.displayName: Rover Quicktune Speed FF to P ratio
RTUN_SPD_P_RATIO.description: Ratio between speed FF and P gain. Raise this to get a higher P gain, 0 to leave P unchanged

RTUN_SPEED_MIN.displayName: Rover Quicktune minimum speed for tuning
RTUN_SPEED_MIN.description: The mimimum speed in m/s required for tuning to start

RTUN_STR_FFRATIO.displayName: Rover Quicktune Steering Rate FeedForward ratio
RTUN_STR_FFRATIO.description: Ratio between measured response and FF gain. Raise this to get a higher FF gain

RTUN_STR_I_RATIO.displayName: Rover Quicktune Steering FF to I ratio
RTUN_STR_I_RATIO.description: Ratio between steering FF and I gains. Raise this to get a higher I gain, 0 to leave I unchanged

RTUN_STR_P_RATIO.displayName: Rover Quicktune Steering FF to P ratio
RTUN_STR_P_RATIO.description: Ratio between steering FF and P gains. Raise this to get a higher P gain, 0 to leave P unchanged

SCHED_DEBUG.description: Set to non-zero to enable scheduler debug messages. When set to show "Slips" the scheduler will display a message whenever a scheduled task is delayed due to too much CPU load. When set to ShowOverruns the scheduled will display a message whenever a task takes longer than the limit promised in the task table.

SCHED_LOOP_RATE.description: This controls the rate of the main control loop in Hz. This should only be changed by developers. This only takes effect on restart. Values over 400 are considered highly experimental.

SCHED_OPTIONS.description: This controls optional aspects of the scheduler.

SCR_DIR_DISABLE.description: This will stop scripts being loaded from the given locations

SCR_HEAP_SIZE.description: Amount of memory available for scripting

SCR_LD_CHECKSUM.description: Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of loaded scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts loaded, -1 disables

SCR_RUN_CHECKSUM.description: Required XOR of CRC32 checksum of running scripts, vehicle will not arm with incorrect scripts running, -1 disables

SCR_SDEV{idx}_PROTO.displayName: Serial protocol of scripting serial device
SCR_SDEV{idx}_PROTO.description: Serial protocol of scripting serial device

SCR_THD_PRIORITY.description: This sets the priority of the scripting thread. This is normally set to a low priority to prevent scripts from interfering with other parts of the system. Advanced users can change this priority if scripting needs to be prioritised for realtime applications. WARNING: changing this parameter can impact the stability of your flight controller. The scipting thread priority in this parameter is chosen based on a set of system level priorities for other subsystems. It is strongly recommended that you use the lowest priority that is sufficient for your application. Note that all scripts run at the same priority, so if you raise this priority you must carefully audit all lua scripts for behaviour that does not interfere with the operation of the system.

SCR_USER1.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER2.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER3.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER4.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER5.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_USER6.description: General purpose user variable input for scripts

SCR_VM_I_COUNT.description: The number virtual machine instructions that can be run before considering a script to have taken an excessive amount of time

SERIAL_PASS1.description: This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port

SERIAL_PASS2.description: This sets one side of pass-through between two serial ports. Once both sides are set then all data received on either port will be passed to the other port

SERIAL_PASSTIMO.description: This sets a timeout for serial pass-through in seconds. When the pass-through is enabled by setting the SERIAL_PASS1 and SERIAL_PASS2 parameters then it remains in effect until no data comes from the first port for SERIAL_PASSTIMO seconds. This allows the port to revent to its normal usage (such as MAVLink connection to a GCS) when it is no longer needed. A value of 0 means no timeout.

SERIAL0_BAUD.description: The baud rate used on the USB console. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL0_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol to use on the console.

SERIAL1_BAUD.description: The baud rate used on the Telem1 port. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL1_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL1_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol to use on the Telem1 port. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL2_BAUD.description: The baud rate of the Telem2 port. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL2_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL2_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol to use on the Telem2 port. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL3_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for the Serial 3 (GPS). Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL3_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL3_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial 3 (GPS) should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL4_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for Serial4. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL4_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL4_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial4 port should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL5_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for Serial5. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL5_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL5_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial5 port should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL6_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for Serial6. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL6_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL6_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial6 port should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL7_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for Serial7. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL7_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL7_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial7 port should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL8_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for Serial8. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL8_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL8_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial8 port should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERIAL9_BAUD.description: The baud rate used for Serial8. Most stm32-based boards can support rates of up to 1500. If you setup a rate you cannot support and then can't connect to your board you should load a firmware from a different vehicle type. That will reset all your parameters to defaults.

SERIAL9_OPTIONS.description: Control over UART options. The InvertRX option controls invert of the receive pin. The InvertTX option controls invert of the transmit pin. The HalfDuplex option controls half-duplex (onewire) mode, where both transmit and receive is done on the transmit wire. The Swap option allows the RX and TX pins to be swapped on STM32F7 based boards.

SERIAL9_PROTOCOL.description: Control what protocol Serial9 port should be used for. Note that the Frsky options require external converter hardware. See the wiki for details.

SERVO_32_ENABLE.description: This allows for up to 32 outputs, enabling parameters for outputs above 16

SERVO_BLH_3DMASK.description: Mask of channels which are dynamically reversible. This is used to configure ESCs in '3D' mode, allowing for the motor to spin in either direction. Do not use for channels selected with SERVO_BLH_RVMASK.

SERVO_BLH_AUTO.displayName: BLHeli pass-thru auto-enable for multicopter motors
SERVO_BLH_AUTO.description: If set to 1 this auto-enables BLHeli pass-thru support for all multicopter motors

SERVO_BLH_BDMASK.displayName: BLHeli bitmask of bi-directional dshot channels
SERVO_BLH_BDMASK.description: Mask of channels which support bi-directional dshot telemetry. This is used for ESCs which have firmware that supports bi-directional dshot allowing fast rpm telemetry values to be returned for the harmonic notch.

SERVO_BLH_DEBUG.description: When set to 1 this enabled verbose debugging output over MAVLink when the blheli protocol is active. This can be used to diagnose failures.

SERVO_BLH_MASK.description: Enable of BLHeli pass-thru servo protocol support to specific channels. This mask is in addition to motors enabled using SERVO_BLH_AUTO (if any)

SERVO_BLH_OTYPE.description: When set to a non-zero value this overrides the output type for the output channels given by SERVO_BLH_MASK. This can be used to enable DShot on outputs that are not part of the multicopter motors group.

SERVO_BLH_POLES.description: This allows calculation of true RPM from ESC's eRPM. The default is 14.

SERVO_BLH_PORT.description: This sets the mavlink channel to use for blheli pass-thru. The channel number is determined by the number of serial ports configured to use mavlink. So 0 is always the console, 1 is the next serial port using mavlink, 2 the next after that and so on.

SERVO_BLH_RVMASK.description: Mask of channels which are reversed. This is used to configure ESCs to reverse motor direction for unidirectional rotation. Do not use for channels selected with SERVO_BLH_3DMASK.

SERVO_BLH_TEST.description: Setting SERVO_BLH_TEST to a motor number enables an internal test of the BLHeli ESC protocol to the corresponding ESC. The debug output is displayed on the USB console.

SERVO_BLH_TMOUT.description: This sets the inactivity timeout for the BLHeli protocol in seconds. If no packets are received in this time normal MAVLink operations are resumed. A value of 0 means no timeout

SERVO_BLH_TRATE.description: This sets the rate in Hz for requesting telemetry from ESCs. It is the rate per ESC. Setting to zero disables telemetry requests

SERVO_DSHOT_ESC.description: DShot ESC type for all outputs. The ESC type affects the range of DShot commands available and the bit widths used. None means that no dshot commands will be executed. Some ESC types support Extended DShot Telemetry (EDT) which allows telemetry other than RPM data to be returned when using bi-directional dshot. If you enable EDT you must install EDT capable firmware for correct operation.

SERVO_DSHOT_RATE.description: DShot output rate for all outputs as a multiple of the loop rate. 0 sets the output rate to be fixed at 1Khz for low loop rates. This value should never be set below 500Hz.

SERVO_FTW_MASK.description: Servo channel mask specifying FETtec ESC output.

SERVO_FTW_RVMASK.description: Servo channel mask to reverse rotation of FETtec ESC outputs.

SERVO_GPIO_MASK.description: Bitmask of outputs which will be available as GPIOs. Any output with either the function set to -1 or with the corresponding bit set in this mask will be available for use as a GPIO pin

SERVO_RATE.description: Default output rate in Hz for all PWM outputs.

SERVO_RC_FS_MSK.description: Bitmask of scaled passthru output channels which will be set to their trim value during rc failsafe instead of holding their last position before failsafe.

SERVO_ROB_POSMAX.description: Position maximum at servo max value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095

SERVO_ROB_POSMIN.description: Position minimum at servo min value. This should be within the position control range of the servos, normally 0 to 4095

SERVO_SBUS_RATE.description: This sets the SBUS output frame rate in Hz.

SERVO_VOLZ_MASK.description: Enable of volz servo protocol to specific channels

SERVO_VOLZ_RANGE.description: Range to map between 1000 and 2000 PWM. Default value of 200 gives full +-100 deg range of extended position command. This results in 0.2 deg movement per US change in PWM. If the full range is not needed it can be reduced to increase resolution. 40 deg range gives 0.04 deg movement per US change in PWM, this is higher resolution than possible with the VOLZ protocol so further reduction in range will not improve resolution. Reduced range does allow PWMs outside the 1000 to 2000 range, with 40 deg range 750 PWM results in a angle of -30 deg, 2250 would be +30 deg. This is still limited by the 200 deg maximum range of the actuator.

SERVO{idx}_FUNCTION.description: Function assigned to this servo. Setting this to Disabled(0) will setup this output for control by auto missions or MAVLink servo set commands. any other value will enable the corresponding function

SERVO{idx}_MAX.description: maximum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO{idx}_MIN.description: minimum PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SERVO{idx}_REVERSED.description: Reverse servo operation. Set to 0 for normal operation. Set to 1 to reverse this output channel.

SERVO{idx}_TRIM.description: Trim PWM pulse width in microseconds. Typically 1000 is lower limit, 1500 is neutral and 2000 is upper limit.

SHIP_AUTO_OFS.description: Settings this parameter to one triggers an automatic follow offset calculation based on current position of the vehicle and the landing target. NOTE: This parameter will auto-reset to zero once the offset has been calculated.

SHIP_LAND_ANGLE.description: Angle from the stern of the ship for landing approach. Use this to ensure that on a go-around that ship superstructure and cables are avoided. A value of zero means to approach from the rear of the ship. A value of 90 means the landing will approach from the port (left) side of the ship. A value of -90 will mean approaching from the starboard (right) side of the ship. A value of 180 will approach from the bow of the ship. This parameter is combined with the sign of the RTL_RADIUS parameter to determine the holdoff pattern. If RTL_RADIUS is positive then a clockwise loiter is performed, if RTL_RADIUS is negative then a counter-clockwise loiter is used.

SID_AXIS.description: Controls which axis are being excited.  Set to non-zero to see more parameters

SID_F_START_HZ.description: Frequency at the start of the sweep

SID_F_STOP_HZ.description: Frequency at the end of the sweep

SID_MAGNITUDE.description: Magnitude of sweep in deg, deg/s and 0-1 for mixer outputs.

SID_T_FADE_IN.description: Time to reach maximum amplitude of sweep

SID_T_FADE_OUT.description: Time to reach zero amplitude at the end of the sweep

SID_T_REC.description: Time taken to complete the sweep
