SIM_THML_SCENARI.description: Scenario for thermalling simulation, for soaring

SIM_TIDE_DIR.description: Tide direction wave is coming from

SIM_TIME_JITTER.description: Upper limit of random jitter in loop time

SIM_TWIST_TIME.description: Time that twist is applied on the vehicle

SIM_TWIST_X.description: Rotational acceleration of twist x axis

SIM_TWIST_Y.description: Rotational acceleration of twist y axis

SIM_TWIST_Z.description: Rotational acceleration of twist z axis

SIM_UART_LOSS.description: Sets percentage of outgoing byte loss on UARTs

SIM_VIB_MOT_MASK.description: Motor mask, allowing external simulators to mark motors

SIM_VIB_MOT_MAX.description: Max frequency to use as baseline for adding motor noise for the gyros and accels

SIM_VIB_MOT_MULT.description: Amplitude scaling of motor noise relative to gyro/accel noise

SIM_VICON_POS_X.displayName: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Forward direction
SIM_VICON_POS_X.description: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Forward direction

SIM_VICON_POS_Y.displayName: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Right direction
SIM_VICON_POS_Y.description: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Right direction

SIM_VICON_POS_Z.displayName: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Down direction
SIM_VICON_POS_Z.description: SITL vicon position on vehicle in Down direction

SIM_VICON_YAW.displayName: SITL vicon yaw angle in earth frame
SIM_VICON_YAW.description: SITL vicon yaw angle in earth frame

SIM_VICON_YAWERR.description: SITL vicon yaw added to reported yaw sent to vehicle

SIM_WIND_DIR.displayName: Direction simulated wind is coming from
SIM_WIND_DIR.description: Allows you to set wind direction (true deg) in sim

SIM_WIND_DIR_Z.description: Allows you to set vertical wind direction (true deg) in sim. 0 means pure horizontal wind. 90 means pure updraft.

SIM_WIND_SPD.description: Allows you to emulate wind in sim

SIM_WIND_T.description: Selects how wind varies from surface to WIND_T_ALT

SIM_WIND_T_ALT.description: Altitude at which wind reaches full strength, decaying from full strength as altitude lowers to ground level

SIM_WIND_T_COEF.description: For linear wind profile,wind is reduced by (Altitude-WIND_T_ALT) x this value

SIM_WIND_TC.description: this controls the time over which wind changes take effect

SIM_WIND_TURB.description: Allows you to emulate random wind variations in sim

SIM_WOW_PIN.description: SITL set this simulated pin to true if vehicle is on ground

SIMPLE.description: Bitmask which holds which flight modes use simple heading mode (eg bit 0 = 1 means Flight Mode 0 uses simple mode). The bitmask is for flightmode switch positions.

SLUP_DEBUG.description: Slung payload debug output, set to 1 to enable debug

SLUP_DIST_MAX.description: Oscillation is suppressed when vehicle and payload are no more than this distance horizontally.  Set to 0 to always suppress

SLUP_RESTOFS_TC.displayName: Slung Payload resting offset estimate filter time constant
SLUP_RESTOFS_TC.description: payload's position estimator's time constant used to compensate for GPS errors and wind.  Higher values result in smoother estimate but slower response

SLUP_SYSID.description: Slung Payload mavlink system id.  0 to use any/all system ids

SLUP_VEL_P.description: Slung Payload Velocity P gain, higher values will result in faster movements in sync with payload

SLUP_WP_POS_P.displayName: Slung Payload return to WP position P gain
SLUP_WP_POS_P.description: WP position P gain. higher values will result in vehicle moving more quickly back to the original waypoint

SPRAY_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) the sprayer

SPRAY_PUMP_MIN.description: Minimum pump speed expressed as a percentage

SPRAY_PUMP_RATE.description: Desired pump speed when travelling 1m/s expressed as a percentage

SPRAY_SPEED_MIN.description: Speed minimum at which we will begin spraying

SPRAY_SPINNER.description: Spinner's rotation speed in PWM (a higher rate will disperse the spray over a wider area horizontally)

SR{idx}_EXT_STAT.description: MAVLink Stream rate of SYS_STATUS, POWER_STATUS, MCU_STATUS, MEMINFO, CURRENT_WAYPOINT, GPS_RAW_INT, GPS_RTK (if available), GPS2_RAW_INT (if available), GPS2_RTK (if available), NAV_CONTROLLER_OUTPUT, FENCE_STATUS, and GLOBAL_TARGET_POS_INT

SR{idx}_EXTRA1.description: MAVLink Stream rate of ATTITUDE, SIMSTATE (SIM only), AHRS2 and PID_TUNING

SR{idx}_EXTRA3.description: MAVLink Stream rate of AHRS, SYSTEM_TIME, WIND, RANGEFINDER, DISTANCE_SENSOR, TERRAIN_REQUEST, TERRAIN_REPORT, BATTERY_STATUS, GIMBAL_DEVICE_ATTITUDE_STATUS, OPTICAL_FLOW, MAG_CAL_REPORT, MAG_CAL_PROGRESS, EKF_STATUS_REPORT, VIBRATION, RPM, ESC TELEMETRY,GENERATOR_STATUS, and WINCH_STATUS

SR{idx}_POSITION.description: MAVLink Stream rate of GLOBAL_POSITION_INT and LOCAL_POSITION_NED

SR{idx}_RAW_SENS.description: MAVLink Stream rate of RAW_IMU, SCALED_IMU2, SCALED_IMU3, SCALED_PRESSURE, SCALED_PRESSURE2, SCALED_PRESSURE3 and AIRSPEED

SR{idx}_RC_CHAN.description: MAVLink Stream rate of SERVO_OUTPUT_RAW and RC_CHANNELS

SRTL_ACCURACY.description: SmartRTL accuracy. The minimum distance between points.

SRTL_POINTS.displayName: SmartRTL maximum number of points on path
SRTL_POINTS.description: SmartRTL maximum number of points on path. Set to 0 to disable SmartRTL.  100 points consumes about 3k of memory.

STAT_BOOTCNT.description: Number of times board has been booted

STAT_RESET.description: Seconds since January 1st 2016 (Unix epoch+1451606400) since statistics reset (set to 0 to reset statistics, other set values will be ignored)

SUPER_SIMPLE.description: Bitmask to enable Super Simple mode for some flight modes. Setting this to Disabled(0) will disable Super Simple Mode. The bitmask is for flight mode switch positions

SURFTRAK_MODE.description: set which surface to track in surface tracking

SURFTRAK_TC.description: Time to achieve 63.2% of the surface altitude measurement change.  If 0 filtering is disabled

SYSID_ENFORCE.description: This controls whether packets from other than the expected GCS system ID will be accepted

SYSID_MYGCS.description: Allows restricting radio overrides to only come from my ground station

SYSID_THISMAV.displayName: MAVLink system ID of this vehicle
SYSID_THISMAV.description: Allows setting an individual MAVLink system id for this vehicle to distinguish it from others on the same network

TA_ACT_FN.description: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for Terrain Avoidance enable/disable

TA_ALT_MAX.description: This is a limit on how high the terrain avoidane will take the vehicle. It acts a failsafe to prevent vertical flyaways.

TA_CMTC_ENABLE.description: Whether to enable Can't Make That Climb while running Terrain Avoidance

TA_CMTC_HGT.description: The minimum Height above terrain to maintain when following an AUTO mission or RTL. If zero(0) use TA_PTCH_DOW_MIN.

TA_CMTC_RAD.description: Use this radius for the loiter when trying to gain altitude. If not set or <=0 use WP_LOITER_RAD

TA_GSP_AIRBRAKE.description: This is the limit for triggering airbrake to slow groundspeed as a difference between the airspeed and groundspeed. -1 for disabled.

TA_GSP_MAX.description: This is a limit on how fast in groundspeeed terrain avoidance will take the vehicle. This is to allow for reliable sensor readings. -1 for disabled.

TA_HOME_DIST.description: Terrain avoidance will not be applied if the vehicle is less than this distance from home

TA_PTCH_DWN_MIN.description: If the downward distance is less than this value then start Pitching up to gain altitude.

TA_PTCH_FWD_MIN.description: If the farwardward distance is less than this value then start Pitching up to gain altitude.

TA_PTCH_GSP_MIN.description: Minimum Groundspeed (not airspeed) to be flying for Pitching to be used.

TA_PTCH_TIMEOUT.description: Minimum down or forward distance must be triggered for more than this many seconds to start Pitching

TA_QUAD_DWN_MIN.description: If the downward distance is less than this value then start Quading up to gain altitude.

TA_QUAD_FWD_MIN.description: If the farwardward distance is less than this value then start Quading up to gain altitude.

TCAL_BARO_EXP.description: Learned exponent for barometer temperature correction

TCAL_ENABLED.description: Enable temperature calibration. Set to 0 to disable. Set to 1 to use learned values. Set to 2 to learn new values and use the values

TCAL_TEMP_MAX.description: Maximum learned temperature. This is automatically set by the learning process

TCAL_TEMP_MIN.description: Minimum learned temperature. This is automatically set by the learning process

TELEM_DELAY.description: The amount of time (in seconds) to delay radio telemetry to prevent an Xbee bricking on power up

values.label: Log only instances with sensor source set to None

TEMP{idx}_A0.description: a0 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP{idx}_A1.description: a1 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP{idx}_A2.description: a2 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP{idx}_A3.description: a3 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP{idx}_A4.description: a4 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP{idx}_A5.description: a5 in polynomial of form temperature in deg = a0 + a1*voltage + a2*voltage^2 + a3*voltage^3 + a4*voltage^4 + a5*voltage^5

TEMP{idx}_ADDR.description: Temperature sensor address, typically used for I2C address

TEMP{idx}_BUS.description: Temperature sensor bus number, typically used to select from multiple I2C buses

TEMP{idx}_PIN.displayName: Temperature sensor analog voltage sensing pin
TEMP{idx}_PIN.description: Sets the analog input pin that should be used for temprature monitoring. Values for some autopilots are given as examples. Search wiki for "Analog pins".

TEMP{idx}_SRC.description: Sensor Source is used to designate which device's temperature report will be replaced by this temperature sensor's data. If 0 (None) then the data is only available via log. In the future a new Motor temperature report will be created for returning data directly.

TEMP{idx}_SRC_ID.description: Sensor Source Identification is used to replace a specific instance of a system component's temperature report with the temp sensor's. Examples: TEMP_SRC = 1 (ESC), TEMP_SRC_ID = 1 will set the temp of ESC1. TEMP_SRC = 3 (BatteryIndex),TEMP_SRC_ID = 2 will set the temp of BATT2. TEMP_SRC = 4 (BatteryId/SerialNum),TEMP_SRC_ID=42 will set the temp of all batteries that have param BATTn_SERIAL = 42.

TERR_BRK_ALT.description: terrain brake altitude. The altitude above the ground below which BRAKE mode will be engaged if in LOITER mode.

TERR_BRK_HDIST.description: terrain brake home distance. The distance from home where the auto BRAKE will be enabled. When within this distance of home the script will not activate

TERR_BRK_SPD.description: terrain brake speed threshold. Don't trigger BRAKE if both horizontal speed and descent rate are below this threshold. By setting this to a small value this can be used to allow the user to climb up to a safe altitude in LOITER mode. A value of 0.5 is recommended if you want to use LOITER to recover from an emergency terrain BRAKE mode change.

TERRAIN_CACHE_SZ.description: The number of 32x28 cache blocks to keep in memory. Each block uses about 1800 bytes of memory

TERRAIN_ENABLE.description: enable terrain data. This enables the vehicle storing a database of terrain data on the SD card. The terrain data is requested from the ground station as needed, and stored for later use on the SD card. To be useful the ground station must support TERRAIN_REQUEST messages and have access to a terrain database, such as the SRTM database.

TERRAIN_MARGIN.description: Margin in centi-meters to accept terrain data from the GCS. This can be used to allow older terrain data generated with less accurate latitude/longitude scaling to be used

TERRAIN_OFS_MAX.description: The maximum adjustment of terrain altitude based on the assumption that the vehicle is on the ground when it is armed. When the vehicle is armed the location of the vehicle is recorded, and when terrain data is available for that location a height adjustment for terrain data is calculated that aligns the terrain height at that location with the altitude recorded at arming. This height adjustment is applied to all terrain data. This parameter clamps the amount of adjustment. A value of zero disables the use of terrain height adjustment.

TERRAIN_OPTIONS.description: Options to change behaviour of terrain system

TERRAIN_SPACING.description: Distance between terrain grid points in meters. This controls the horizontal resolution of the terrain data that is stored on te SD card and requested from the ground station. If your GCS is using the ArduPilot SRTM database like Mission Planner or MAVProxy, then a resolution of 100 meters is appropriate. Grid spacings lower than 100 meters waste SD card space if the GCS cannot provide that resolution. The grid spacing also controls how much data is kept in memory during flight. A larger grid spacing will allow for a larger amount of data in memory. A grid spacing of 100 meters results in the vehicle keeping 12 grid squares in memory with each grid square having a size of 2.7 kilometers by 3.2 kilometers. Any additional grid squares are stored on the SD once they are fetched from the GCS and will be loaded as needed.

THR_DZ.description: The deadzone above and below mid throttle in PWM microseconds. Used in AltHold, Loiter, PosHold flight modes

THROW_ALT_MAX.description: Maximum altitude under which Throw mode will detect a throw or a drop - 0 to disable the check

THROW_ALT_MIN.description: Minimum altitude above which Throw mode will detect a throw or a drop - 0 to disable the check

THROW_MOT_START.displayName: Start motors before throwing is detected
THROW_MOT_START.description: Used by Throw mode. Controls whether motors will run at the speed set by MOT_SPIN_MIN or will be stopped when armed and waiting for the throw.

THROW_NEXTMODE.description: Vehicle will switch to this mode after the throw is successfully completed.  Default is to stay in throw mode (18)

THROW_TYPE.description: Used by Throw mode. Specifies whether Copter is thrown upward or dropped.

TKOFF_RPM_MAX.description: Takeoff is not permitted until motors report no more than this RPM.  Set to zero to disable check

TKOFF_RPM_MIN.description: Takeoff is not permitted until motors report at least this RPM.  Set to zero to disable check

TKOFF_SLEW_TIME.displayName: Slew time of throttle during take-off
TKOFF_SLEW_TIME.description: Time to slew the throttle from minimum to maximum while checking for a succsessful takeoff.

TKOFF_THR_MAX.displayName: Takeoff maximum throttle during take-off ramp up
TKOFF_THR_MAX.description: Takeoff maximum throttle allowed before controllers assume the aircraft is airborne during the takeoff process.

TMODE_ACTION1.description: This is the action taken when the left action button is pressed

TMODE_ACTION2.description: This is the action taken when the right action button is pressed

TMODE_ACTION3.description: This is the action taken when the power button is pressed

TMODE_ACTION4.description: This is the action taken when the left action button is pressed while the left (Mode) button is held down

TMODE_ACTION5.description: This is the action taken when the right action is pressed while the left (Mode) button is held down

TMODE_ACTION6.description: This is the action taken when the power button is pressed while the left (Mode) button is held down

TMODE_ENABLE.description: tmode (or "toy" mode) gives a simplified user interface designed for mass market drones. Version1 is for the SkyViper V2450GPS. Version2 is for the F412 based boards

TMODE_FLAGS.description: Bitmask of flags to change the behaviour of tmode. DisarmOnLowThrottle means to disarm if throttle is held down for 1 second when landed. ArmOnHighThrottle means to arm if throttle is above 80% for 1 second. UpgradeToLoiter means to allow takeoff in LOITER mode by switching to ALT_HOLD, then auto-upgrading to LOITER once GPS is available. RTLStickCancel means that on large stick inputs in RTL mode that LOITER mode is engaged

TMODE_LEFT.description: This is the action taken when the left (Mode) button is pressed

TMODE_LEFT_LONG.description: This is the action taken when the left (Mode) button is long-pressed

TMODE_LOAD_FILT.description: This filters the load test output. A value of 1 means no filter. 2 means values are repeated once. 3 means values are repeated 3 times, etc

TMODE_LOAD_MUL.description: This scales the load test output, as a value between 0 and 1

TMODE_LOAD_TYPE.description: This sets the type of load test

TMODE_MODE1.description: This is the initial mode when the vehicle is first turned on. This mode is assumed to not require GPS

TMODE_MODE2.description: This is the secondary mode. This mode is assumed to require GPS

TMODE_RIGHT.description: This is the action taken when the right (Return) button is pressed

TMODE_TMAX.description: This sets the thrust multiplier when voltage is low

TMODE_TMIN.description: This sets the thrust multiplier when voltage is high

TMODE_TRIM_AUTO.description: This is the amount of automatic stick trim that can be applied when disarmed with sticks not moving. It is a PWM limit value away from 1500

TMODE_VMAX.description: This is the battery voltage above which thrust min is used

TMODE_VMIN.description: This is the battery voltage below which no output limiting is done

TOFSENSE_ID1.description: First TOFSENSE-M sensor ID. Leave this at 0 to accept all IDs and if only one sensor is present. You can change ID of sensor from NAssistant Software

TOFSENSE_ID2.description: Second TOFSENSE-M sensor ID. This cannot be 0. You can change ID of sensor from NAssistant Software

TOFSENSE_ID3.description: Third TOFSENSE-M sensor ID. This cannot be 0. You can change ID of sensor from NAssistant Software

TOFSENSE_INST1.description: First TOFSENSE-M sensors backend Instance. Setting this to 1 will pick the first backend from PRX_ or RNG_ Parameters (Depending on TOFSENSE_PRX)

TOFSENSE_INST2.description: Second TOFSENSE-M sensors backend Instance. Setting this to 2 will pick the second backend from PRX_ or RNG_ Parameters (Depending on TOFSENSE_PRX)

TOFSENSE_INST3.description: Third TOFSENSE-M sensors backend Instance. Setting this to 3 will pick the second backend from PRX_ or RNG_ Parameters (Depending on TOFSENSE_PRX)

TOFSENSE_MODE.description: TOFSENSE-M mode to be used. 0 for 8x8 mode. 1 for 4x4 mode

TOFSENSE_NO.description: Number of TOFSENSE-M CAN sensors connected

TOFSENSE_PRX.displayName: TOFSENSE-M to be used as Proximity sensor
TOFSENSE_PRX.description: Set 0 if sensor is to be used as a 1-D rangefinder (minimum of all distances will be sent, typically used for height detection). Set 1 if it should be used as a 3-D proximity device (Eg. Obstacle Avoidance)

TOFSENSE_S1_BR.description: Serial Port baud rate. Sensor baud rate can be changed from Nassistant software

TOFSENSE_S1_PRX.displayName: TOFSENSE-M to be used as Proximity sensor
TOFSENSE_S1_PRX.description: Set 0 if sensor is to be used as a 1-D rangefinder (minimum of all distances will be sent, typically used for height detection). Set 1 if it should be used as a 3-D proximity device (Eg. Obstacle Avoidance)

TOFSENSE_S1_SP.description: UART instance sensor is connected to. Set 1 if sensor is connected to the port with fist SERIALx_PROTOCOL = 28.

TRIK_ACT_FN.description: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for trick action (abort,announce,execute)

TRIK_COUNT.description: Number of tricks which can be selected over the range of the trik selection RC channel

TRIK_ENABLE.description: Enables Tricks on Switch. TRIK params hidden until enabled

TRIK_SEL_FN.description: Setting an RC channel's _OPTION to this value will use it for trick selection

TUNE.description: Controls which parameters (normally PID gains) are being tuned with transmitter's channel 6 knob

TUNE_MAX.description: Transmitter Tuning maximum value. The parameter being tuned will have its value set to this maximum value when the tuning knob is at its highest position

TUNE_MIN.description: Transmitter Tuning minum value. The parameter being tuned will have its value set to this minimum value when the tuning knob is at its lowest position

VID1_IPADDR0.description: Video stream IP Address first octet

VID1_IPADDR1.description: Video stream IP Address second octet

VID1_IPADDR2.description: Video stream IP Address third octet

VID1_IPADDR3.description: Video stream IP Address fourth octet

VID1_IPPORT.displayName: Camera1 Video Stream IP Address Port

VIEP_CAM_SWHIGH.description: Camera selection when switch is in high position

VIEP_CAM_SWLOW.description: Camera selection when switch is in low position

VIEP_CAM_SWMID.description: Camera selection when switch is in middle position

VIEP_ZOOM_SPEED.description: ViewPro Zoom Speed.  Higher numbers result in faster zooming

VISO_DELAY_MS.description: Visual odometry sensor delay relative to inertial measurements

VISO_POS_M_NSE.description: Visual odometry position measurement noise minimum (meters). This value will be used if the sensor provides a lower noise value (or no noise value)

VISO_POS_X.displayName: Visual odometry camera X position offset
VISO_POS_X.description: X position of the camera in body frame. Positive X is forward of the origin.

VISO_POS_Y.displayName: Visual odometry camera Y position offset
VISO_POS_Y.description: Y position of the camera in body frame. Positive Y is to the right of the origin.

VISO_POS_Z.displayName: Visual odometry camera Z position offset
VISO_POS_Z.description: Z position of the camera in body frame. Positive Z is down from the origin.

VISO_QUAL_MIN.description: Visual odometry will only be sent to EKF if over this value. -1 to always send (even bad values), 0 to send if good or unknown

VISO_SCALE.description: Visual odometry scaling factor applied to position estimates from sensor

VISO_VEL_M_NSE.description: Visual odometry velocity measurement noise in m/s

VISO_YAW_M_NSE.description: Visual odometry yaw measurement noise minimum (radians), This value will be used if the sensor provides a lower noise value (or no noise value)

VTX_ENABLE.displayName: Is the Video Transmitter enabled or not
VTX_ENABLE.description: Toggles the Video Transmitter on and off

VTX_FREQ.description: Video Transmitter Frequency. The frequency is derived from the setting of BAND and CHANNEL

VTX_MAX_POWER.description: Video Transmitter Maximum Power Level. Different VTXs support different power levels, this prevents the power aux switch from requesting too high a power level. The switch supports 6 power levels and the selected power will be a subdivision between 0 and this setting.

VTX_OPTIONS.description: Video Transmitter Options. Pitmode puts the VTX in a low power state. Unlocked enables certain restricted frequencies and power levels. Do not enable the Unlocked option unless you have appropriate permissions in your jurisdiction to transmit at high power levels. One stop-bit may be required for VTXs that erroneously mimic iNav behaviour.

VTX_POWER.description: Video Transmitter Power Level. Different VTXs support different power levels, the power level chosen will be rounded down to the nearest supported power level

WEB_BLOCK_SIZE.description: web server block size for download

WEB_SENDFILE_MIN.displayName: web server minimum file size for sendfile
WEB_SENDFILE_MIN.description: sendfile is an offloading mechanism for faster file download. If this is non-zero and the file is larger than this size then sendfile will be used for file download

WINCH_POS_P.displayName: Winch control position error P gain
WINCH_POS_P.description: Winch control position error P gain

WINCH_RATE_MAX.displayName: Winch deploy or retract rate maximum
WINCH_RATE_MAX.description: Winch deploy or retract rate maximum.  Set to maximum rate with no load.

WINCH_RC_FUNC.description: RCn_OPTION number to use to control winch rate

WP_NAVALT_MIN.description: This is the altitude in meters above which for navigation can begin. This applies in auto takeoff and auto landing.

WP_YAW_BEHAVIOR.description: Determines how the autopilot controls the yaw during missions and RTL

WPNAV_ACCEL.description: Defines the horizontal acceleration in cm/s/s used during missions

WPNAV_ACCEL_C.description: Defines the maximum cornering acceleration in cm/s/s used during missions.  If zero uses 2x accel value.

WPNAV_ACCEL_Z.description: Defines the vertical acceleration in cm/s/s used during missions

WPNAV_JERK.description: Defines the horizontal jerk in m/s/s used during missions

WPNAV_RADIUS.description: Defines the distance from a waypoint, that when crossed indicates the wp has been hit.

WPNAV_RFND_USE.displayName: Waypoint missions use rangefinder for terrain following
WPNAV_RFND_USE.description: This controls if waypoint missions use rangefinder for terrain following

WPNAV_SPEED.description: Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain horizontally during a WP mission

WPNAV_SPEED_DN.description: Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain while descending during a WP mission

WPNAV_SPEED_UP.description: Defines the speed in cm/s which the aircraft will attempt to maintain while climbing during a WP mission

WPNAV_TER_MARGIN.description: Waypoint Terrain following altitude margin.  Vehicle will stop if distance from target altitude is larger than this margin (in meters)

WVANE_ANG_MIN.description: The minimum target roll/pitch angle before active weathervaning will start.  This provides a dead zone that is particularly useful for poorly trimmed quadplanes.

WVANE_ENABLE.description: Enable weather vaning.  When active, the aircraft will automatically yaw into wind when in a VTOL position controlled mode. Pilot yaw commands override the weathervaning action.

values.label: Only use during takeoffs or landing see weathervane takeoff and land override parameters

WVANE_GAIN.description: This converts the target roll/pitch angle of the aircraft into the correcting (into wind) yaw rate. e.g. Gain = 2, roll = 30 deg, pitch = 0 deg, yaw rate = 60 deg/s.

WVANE_HGT_MIN.description: Above this height weathervaning is permitted.  If a range finder is fitted or if terrain is enabled, this parameter sets height AGL.  Otherwise, this parameter sets height above home.  Set zero to ignore minimum height requirement to activate weathervaning.

WVANE_LAND.description: Override the weather vaning behaviour when in landing

WVANE_SPD_MAX.description: Below this ground speed weathervaning is permitted. Set to 0 to ignore this condition when checking if vehicle should weathervane.

WVANE_TAKEOFF.description: Override the weather vaning behaviour when in takeoffs

WVANE_VELZ_MAX.description: The maximum climb or descent speed that the vehicle will still attempt to weathervane. Set to 0 to ignore this condition to get the aircraft to weathervane at any climb/descent rate.  This is particularly useful for aircraft with low disc loading that struggle with yaw control in decent.

ZIGZ_AUTO_ENABLE.description: Allows you to enable (1) or disable (0) ZigZag auto feature

ZIGZ_DIRECTION.description: The direction to move sideways in ZigZag mode

ZIGZ_LINE_NUM.description: Total number of lines for ZigZag auto if 1 or more. -1: Infinity, 0: Just moving to sideways

ZIGZ_SIDE_DIST.description: The distance to move sideways in ZigZag mode

ZIGZ_SPRAYER.description: Enable the auto sprayer in ZigZag mode. SPRAY_ENABLE = 1 and SERVOx_FUNCTION = 22(SprayerPump) / 23(SprayerSpinner) also must be set. This makes the sprayer on while moving to destination A or B. The sprayer will stop if the vehicle reaches destination or the flight mode is changed from ZigZag to other.

ZIGZ_WP_DELAY.description: Waiting time after reached the destination
